title: The One Where No One Proposes

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s Room, Joey moves Ross’s coat to get the tissues Rachel wants and the engagement ring box Mrs. Geller gave him falls out of the pocket it was inside. Joey goes to one knee, picks up the box, opens it, and sees that it’s an engagement ring.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God. Okay.

scenes.scene_description: He turns to face Rachel on one knee with the box open.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So uh…I guess we should…make it official huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh… Look Rach… Hey Ross is here! Hey look! It’s my good friend Ross. Hey Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Joey. Hey you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey and look he brought flowers. Thanks Ross, but I’m really more of a candy guy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You’re weird today. Listen I uh, wanted to talk to you about something.
lines.stage_direction: He turns to Rachel and Joey puts the ring back. To Rachel

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh yeah, actually I kinda need to talk to you too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh Joey, can you give us just a minute?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I’m sorry. I meant no.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi! Hey look who’s here!
lines.stage_direction: entering with everyone else including Mr. Geller

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Where’s my granddaughter? I’ve been practicing my magic tricks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He pulled a quarter out of my ear!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, where’s uh, where’s mom?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: She went to pick up Aunt Liddy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, Aunt Liddy’s coming? That means we get five dollars each!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: So when do I get to meet Emma and show her this?
lines.stage_direction: Pulls a bouquet of flowers out of his sleeve.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Wow.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh Dad, Emma’s in the nursery. I’ll take you now. If you want, but I really want to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, I still need to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh hey but, before you guys do that I need to talk to you, and Ross, I need to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh and I need to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: About what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: To see if know what these guys are talking about.

scenes.scene_description: Joey is stunned.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Isn’t she beautiful?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Look at her, my first grandchild.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What about Ben?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well of course Ben, I meant my first granddaughter. Wow.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Have umm, have you thought anymore about you and Rachel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh well yeah, actually I was going to talk to her when you guys all came in the room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yay! It’s so exciting! Wow, you could’ve done that with us there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh sure okay, you can touch yourself in front of us but you can’t talk to Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! When have I ever touched myself in front of you guys?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh please! Just before when you were asleep in the lounge! That Armenian family was watching you instead of the TV. Oh, that reminds me. That Mr. Hasmeje still has my Gameboy .

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Chandler, can I talk to you for a second?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude I just did something terrible.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was you?! I thought it was Jack!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No, that was Jack! Rachel thinks I asked her to marry me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?! Why does she think that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Because it kinda looked like I did.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Again, what?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay well, I was down on one knee with the ring in my hand…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: As we all are at some point during the day.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It wasn’t my ring! It fell out of Ross’s jacket! And when I knelt down to pick it up Rachel thought I was proposing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross had a ring?! And he was gonna propose?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I guess.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And you did it first?! This is gonna kill him! You know how much he loves to propose!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know! I know it’s awful.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, what did she say?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She said yes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Does Ross know?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh God, what the hell am I going to tell him?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well maybe you don’t have to tell him anything.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I like that. Yeah…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If you clear things up with Rachel then Ross never needs to find out, but you have to do it now before he hears about it and kicks your ass!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now let’s not get carried away.
lines.stage_direction: laughs He walks away as Monica comes over and hugs Chandler from behind.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I want a baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, we’ve been over this. I need to be facing the other way.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on! Come on, if we have sex again it’ll double our chances of getting pregnant. Do you think that closet’s still available?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m so tired. Yeah okay, but no foreplay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Deal!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad seriously! Y’know you really should see someone about that!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Noted.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I wanna go talk to Rachel for a minute, are you gonna be okay alone for a bit?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Are you kidding me, I could stay and look at her forever.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Actually umm…
lines.stage_direction: noticing something He turns Mr. Geller’s head to look at Emma.

scenes.scene_description: Outside the Nursery, everyone but Rachel is standing and looking into the window.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you all right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uhh… I think I just got engaged.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! He did it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well…did you know he was gonna ask me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you kidding? I’m like the one who talked him into it. I like to think of myself as the puppet master of the group.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And you really think this is a good idea?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just talked him into it, don’t tell me I have to do you too. The puppet master gets tired people.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just don’t know! It just doesn’t feel right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why?! You two are so meant to be together, everybody thinks so.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?! Even Ross?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Especially Ross!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh uh, hey Pheebs. Uh y’know what? I’ll-I’ll come back later.
lines.stage_direction: entering He goes to leave but runs into Ross who’s entering.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! Kind of uh, kind of a full house here. I’ll guess just…I’ll come back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There he goes, your fianc�e.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I guess so.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Although he does play with himself in his sleep.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can’t say that I’m surprised.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s Room, Phoebe is entering.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I think there are people in there having sex.

scenes.scene_description: A hallway, Joey and Ross find Mr. Geller with his ear up against a janitor’s closet door.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It can’t be me, I’m standing right here.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Wanna peek?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Come on!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know what? I don’t like you without mom. Come on.

scenes.scene_description: Ross turns to look at Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We’re not peeking?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well I’m peeking. Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello sir, you know Monica.

scenes.scene_description: Walks away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey uh, is it okay to come in?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Of course! Oh Joey, this ring I…it’s beautiful I love it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah uh look Rach, there’s something I gotta tell ya.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s Room, she is taking the ring out of Ross’s jacket, looks at it, and puts it on her finger as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Hey! Are you ready to try nursing again?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! Hi Emma. Hey, why do you think she won’t take my breast?

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: It’s all right honey, it takes some babies a while to get it, but don’t worry. It’ll happen.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yowsa!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay sweetie, you can do it. Just open up and put it in your mouth.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dear Lord.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m sorry honey, what were you saying?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh uh-uh yeah, I think that…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh look, she’s pulling away again! Do you think my nipples are too big for her mouth? She looks scared. Doesn’t she look scared?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y’know, I don’t really know her.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Why don’t we try massaging the breast to stimulate the flow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you kidding me?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It’s just so frustrating! Why doesn’t she want my breast?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know! Maybe she’s crazy!

scenes.scene_description: There’s a knock on the door and a nurse enters carrying Emma.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh hey! Wait up!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Congratulations! I didn’t want to say anything in front of Joey ‘cause I didn’t know if he knew yet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, that we had a baby? Come on let’s give him a little credit, although, he did eat a piece of plastic fruit earlier.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! No, that you and Rachel are engaged!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it’s a secret. Oh goodie! Yes! We haven’t done the secret thing in a long time.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, there is no secret. Okay? I didn’t propose.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you lying? Is this like that time you tried to convince us that you were a doctor?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am a doctor! Y’know what? I’m just gonna go and talk to Rachel myself.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, me too. Should we wake her up?
lines.stage_direction: They go into her room and see that she’s sleeping.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No, come on let her sleep! She’s so exhausted.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And so engaged.
lines.stage_direction: Points to the ring that Rachel is wearing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Oh my God! She-she thinks we’re engaged! Why? Why? Why would she think we’re engaged?!
lines.stage_direction: Motions for Phoebe to go outside with him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Perhaps because you gave her an engagement ring? Y’know Ross doctors are supposed to be smart.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I didn’t give her that ring!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So whose ring is it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s mine.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Is it an engagement ring?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But you didn’t give it to her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But you were going to propose?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh, I might be losing interest in this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look. Look, my mom gave me that ring because she wanted me to propose to Rachel, but all I wanted to do is if she maybe…kinda…wanted ah…start…things up again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, what beautiful lukewarm sentiment.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I didn’t want to rush into anything. And it seemed like she didn’t want to either. But I don’t, I don’t understand how any of this happened! What? Did she find the ring in my jacket, assume that I was going to propose, throw it on, and-and just start telling people?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! No, she said you actually proposed to her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I didn’t! I didn’t propose! Unless uh… Did I? I haven’t slept in forty hours and…it does sound like something I would do.

scenes.scene_description: The Lobby, Ross is eating a sandwich as Phoebe rushes up to him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, we can’t stay in here forever.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I still can’t believe my dad saw us having sex! He didn’t make it to one of piano recitals, but this he sees!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is okay. We’re all adults here; there’s nothing to be ashamed of. Now, let’s put our underwear in our pockets and walk out the door.
lines.stage_direction: They do so and find Mr. Geller leaning against a wall stunned.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi Dad! I can still call you that right?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Of course. I’ll always be your dad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I just want you to know that what you witnessed in there, that wasn’t for fun.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It wasn’t fun?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why? Why-why-would you—Wh-why… Look, I just don’t want you to think that we’re animals who do it whenever we want.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Oh, I don’t think that. Before today I never thought of you two having sex at all. It was a simpler time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The truth is, Dad, we’re-we’re trying.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, we’re trying to get pregnant.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Oh my God! This is so exciting! Well, get back in there! I’ll guard the door!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, that’s okay dad, we-we can wait until later.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa! I don’t think so! Aren’t you ovulating?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Daddy?!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well you gotta get at it princess! When your mother and I were trying to conceive you, whenever she was ovulating, bam, we did it. That’s how I got my bad hip.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s funny, this conversation’s how I got the bullet hole in my head.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: This one time I had my knee up on the sink and your mother, she was…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Daddy! I don’t think we need to hear about the specific positions you and mom had sex.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: You’re right, you’re right. This is about your positions. Now, what I saw in the closet is not the optimum position for conceiving a child, although it might feel good.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t feel good right now.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: But pleasure is important, and it helps if the woman has an orgasm. You up to the task sailor?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Seriously sir, my brains? All over the wall.

scenes.scene_description: The Janitor’s Closet, Chandler and Monica are trying to figure out what to do now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey. I need to tell you something.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, now’s a good time. I’m on my way to have my ears cut off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey asked me marry him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey proposed to me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is he crazy?! You just had Ross’s baby!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I-I said yes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! Are you crazy? You just had Ross’s baby! It’s-it’s so inappropriate. No, it’s worse than that. It’s wrong. It’s… It is bigger than mine!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know. Days of Our Lives , thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You can’t marry him!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why not? I don’t want to do this alone! And he’s such a sweet guy and he loves me so much.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well do you love him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sure?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I mean whatever.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, the question is…do you really want to marry Joey?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. No, I don’t. Could you be a dear and go tell him?

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s Room, Monica is entering.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You still haven’t told Rachel you weren’t really proposing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! She had the ring on, she seemed so excited, and then she took her breast out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey, you have to tell her what’s going on! And what did it look like?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I didn’t look at it. Stupid baby’s head was blocking most of it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Go and tell Rachel right now before Ross finds out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, it’s not that easy. She said she wanted to marry me. I don’t want to hurt her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, look, just do it gently.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You’re right. You’re right. I-I’ll go tell her now before Ross finds out and I’ll be gentle. I can do that. I am a gentle person. Oh, by the way. Two people screwing in there if you want to check that out.
lines.stage_direction: Points to the closet Chandler and Monica were in

scenes.scene_description: A Hallway, Chandler is following Joey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Listen, I um… I heard about the engagement.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Surprised?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And confused. Rach, sweetie, I-I um…I didn’t propose to you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don’t think you do.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You didn’t propose to me. Joey did.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Poor baby, you’re so tired. Rach, I didn’t propose to you, Joey didn’t propose to you, and Chandler didn’t propose to you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh… You didn’t propose to me, Chandler didn’t propose to me, but Joey did.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s Room, Ross is entering.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey proposed to you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can come back.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, wait! Wait-wait-wait! Joey, did you propose to her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes you did!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Actually, technically, I didn’t.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well then why did you give me a ring?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait! Whoa-whoa, you…you gave her the ring?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No, and I did not ask her to marry me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, you did!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I didn’t!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, you did!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I didn’t!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, you did! And don’t you say, "No, I didn’t!"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahhh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He was right there. He got down on one knee and proposed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa! You were down on one knee?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, that looks bad. But I didn’t…I didn’t propose!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then what did happen?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, what did happen?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, the ring fell on the floor and I went down to pick it up and you thought I was proposing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but you said, "Will you marry me?"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I didn’t!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, you did!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I didn’t!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, you did—Oh my God you didn’t! Well then why didn’t you tell me that before?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I tried, but people kept coming in and then you took your breast out!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa! Hey! Whoa-whoa-whoa, you saw her breast?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’ll tell you about it later. Be cool.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well then Joey, what the hell were you doing with an engagement ring?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It wasn’t my ring! It’s Ross’s ring! That’s why I felt so bad Rach, because he was going to propose.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You were gonna propose to me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhh… No.

scenes.scene_description: Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, this is awkward. {See? I told you so.}

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But I-I was going to see if y’know, maybe you uh, start dating again but that—I mean that-that was all, Rach.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, step up! I proposed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, you didn’t!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh that’s right. There’s a lot going on here and I think I ate some bad fruit earlier.

scenes.scene_description: An awkward silence follows.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Hey, she just woke up! She’s hungry. Why don’t we give this another try?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can’t believe you told her I was going to propose!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can’t believe you’re not going to propose!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I’m not going to rush into anything!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, dude, I totally understand. Usually after I have a baby with a woman I like to slow things down!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She’s doing it Look, she’s breast-feeding look!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, it’s beautiful.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: I’ll come back for her later.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow, this feels weird.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good weird?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wonderful weird.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y’know what you guys? I’m uh, I’m gonna go too. And uh, I’m sorry about everything.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey don’t worry, it was my mistake.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, Rach, I should’ve told you sooner. It’s just that…Man! That kid is going to town!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She’s perfect.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We’re so lucky.

lines.speaker: Rachel

lines.text: I know!

lines.text: You need both hands for that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aaahhh, look at you two... holding hands... huh is this getting serious? Have you not talked about it yet? Am I making you uncomfortable? If you were bigger you'd hit me, huh...? Aaaaaahhhhhh
lines.stage_direction: in a very aaaaahhhhh sweeeeeet voice Phoebe and Mike, embarrassed, start babbling and look away. They say nothing now, but smile smiles are becoming forced now, and he speaks to Mike he turns away to the bar

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry... I'm sorry. It's obviously way too early for us to be... having that conversation.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Is it?

lines.text: Maybe not, is it?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Okay, when I got divorced, I didn't think I'd feel this way about someone for a really long time... Then again, I didn't think I'd meet someone like you... and... this may be crazy soon, but... I want you to have this... No, not... that's gum. Ooh, five bucks... I love it when that happens, you know... Think no note's there...
lines.stage_direction: He tries to get something from his pocket, but it's not that easy... Phoebe looks in a "what's happening" face to him. He finally has found something He digs in a little more.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know Mike, why don't you keep digging?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh, sorry.
lines.stage_direction: He digs in again and finally finds what he's been looking for. A key.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's a key. To be honest, I think I'd prefer the five dollars.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: It's to my apartment.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh wow, ooh! Ooh, big step for Phoebe and Mike.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah, look, and I don't want you to feel like you have to give me your key just because...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, I want to.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh, thank God.
lines.stage_direction: he laughs nervously, and Phoebe gets her keychain from her bag.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah... ooh... wow... Even started to think I'd never meet someone that, you know, I wanted to... do this with. Here you go.

lines.text: Is this cool, huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It really is.

lines.text: Oh, I know it... It is amazing these little things open doors... huh!

lines.text: Well... hey, the key works...!
lines.stage_direction: he looks as if he doesn't want to believe what's happening

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And you thanks for the face massage. Thank you.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe's apartment. She and David are still kissing each other in the living room.

scenes.location: Monica and Chandler's apartment. Monica is in the kitchen and Ross enters

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did Rachel tell you we hired a male nanny?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! I think that's great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh really? Did she tell you he plays the recorder, recites poetry and bakes Madeleines?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh... How are they?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Lighter than air... But that's not the point.
lines.stage_direction: looks like in heaven changes back to serious Joey now also enters

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey...! Rachel and I hired a male nanny.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really...? Guys do that...? That's... weird...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's like a woman wanting to be a...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...a what? A what? What's the end of that sentence?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes... What is the end of that sentence?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhm... A penis model. Anyway, hey... Did you tell Chandler that some guy from work is the funniest guy you've ever met ?
lines.stage_direction: Monica can't believe what's she's hearing and Ross pats Joey on the back.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, so?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? Do you not know Chandler?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is that why he's acting so weird...? He's jealous...? Oh my God, that is crazy. It's not like I'm attracted to Geoffrey...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So what? Being funny is Chandler's thing ... You know, like Ross's thing is...
lines.stage_direction: he can't come up with anything

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Science...? Academia...? Being a good father...?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.stage_direction: he just can't seem to grasp it

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe he's that upset about this...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Monica, you have to do some damage control here, okay. 'Cause he's feeling like...
lines.stage_direction: the door opens and Chandler walks in with a pizza

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Heeeyy! Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you guys talking about?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhm... Rachel and I hired a male nanny.
lines.stage_direction: Joey makes a gesture and sound like "Can you believe that?"

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You got a man who's a nanny...? You got a manny...?
lines.stage_direction: Monica starts laughing, but very exaggerated. Joey realises they also should laugh and punches Ross. Now all three of them laugh, but very fake. Chandler seems happy again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I don't mind a... male nanny, but I do draw the line at a male wetnurse.
lines.stage_direction: again they laugh, even more fake than before

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohhh, ooohhh... you are on a roll, mister!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If I'd known you guys were coming over, I would have brought more pizza.
lines.stage_direction: they all burst out in a thundering laugh

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, okay... Chandler you... you stop it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What is so funny about that ?
lines.stage_direction: they realise it wasn't a joke

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I don't know... I-It's... just the way you say it... I mean, you're funny... You have that funny thing. You're a funny guy!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's apartment. Monica is in the kitchen and Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did you tell her what we talked about?
lines.stage_direction: Joey starts laughing hysterically, but then gets serious again...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah....

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So those were pity laughs? PITY LAUGHS?
lines.stage_direction: Joey and Ross walk away from the kitchen

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, listen... You have nothing to worry about with Geoffrey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah? Is he funnier than me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, you're... you're different funny... I mean, you're... you're more sarcastic a-a-and... well, he does... bits... and impressions... and... and limericks...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I do limericks... uhm... There once was a man named Chandler, whose wife made him die inside.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, you know I think you're hilarious! Come on, you know that joke you told me last week? The one about Jerry Lewis and the girl with the lazy eye...? That slayed me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey... I made up that joke and told it to you!
lines.stage_direction: He points at Chandler. Joey gestures to Ross "What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Not knowing when to shut up...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yep! That's my thing...

scenes.scene_description: Chandler turns to Joey

scenes.location: Phoebe's apartment. David is still there. Mike closes the door

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: So... how many guys have your key?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, no, no, no... It's not... it's not... i'ts not as bad as it looks... really. I was just saying goodbye to an old friend.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Your lipstick's on his mouth.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Oh, uh... we just uh... happen to wear the same shade.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, uhm... David and I did use to go out... but years ago, and he lives in Minsk. He's only... he's only in town for a couple of days.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Did you uhm...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no...

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: ...kiss him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, well, yeah...

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yes, but uhm... You should know... she really likes you. I-In fact I-I-I don't think you realise j-just how lucky you are fella.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe's apartment. David is still there. Mike closes the door.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Don't point your finger at me .

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Why? Wh-What are you going to do about it?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well... I'll... just show you what I'm gonna do about it...
lines.stage_direction: he hits David's finger with his finger and they start to finger-fight using their fingers as swords saying all kinds of macho crap

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Stop it! Stop it, before someone gets really hurt! Here David, you should just go.
lines.stage_direction: they stop and Phoebe gets David's jacket and gives it to him

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Allright... But... if I ever do come back from Minsk... well, you just better watch out.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well, if I ever go to Minsk, you 'd better watch out.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Oh, you're going to Minsk?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well, I might.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Really? Well, if you do, come in the spring. It's just lovely there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well... guys?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Right... Goodbye Phoebe.
lines.stage_direction: Makes a move to kiss her.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hey, what are you kidding me?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Right-o, right-o... Take good care of her.

scenes.scene_description: he points at Mike

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'm so sorry. If you... If you want your key back, I totally understand.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: It's never gonna happen again right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Right! Never! Never! I swear!
lines.stage_direction: They kiss... The door opens and David comes in again.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I-I... Oh I...I just wanna say uhm... if you do ever come to Minsk, that's my number We'll uhm... we'll party up Vladnik style.
lines.stage_direction: gives Mike a business card He leaves again

scenes.scene_description: and he leaves

scenes.scene_description: Ross goes to the kitchen

lines.text: That was kind of rude!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry. Please apologise to Sandy and the Snufflebumps for me.

lines.text: You know, he was just doing his job...

lines.text: Well, you know what... I-I'm sorry I'm the only one who isn't in love with Gary Poppins out there... But I just... I can't... I can't go through with this.

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I'm off to Tulsa, so if your Maitre D. friend has any funny Oklahoma jokes, tell him to e-mail me at www.hahanotsomuch.com .

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, you can relax. Last night at work, Geoffrey told this really sexist joke. After that, not so funny anymore.

lines.text: Really...? See... that's the thing: you gotta keep it smart, people!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, don't miss that flight. You know I love you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I love you too. And... I like you as a friend.

lines.text: Allright. See you later!

lines.text: See ya!

lines.text: Did that guy really make that joke?

lines.speaker: Monica

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here goes...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't watch. It's like firing Elmo.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sandy... Hi, we uhm... we kinda need to talk. I'm afraid it's not working out.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, uhm... I mean, Rachel and I, think you are great... with Emma... uhm... We just feel...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: YOU! You feel!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I... just feel that the... the chemistry isn't right. I'm sorry. We're... we're more than happy to give you good recommendation...

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Oh, no, no, no... That's okay. I got a lot of offers from other families. I just picked you guys because... I liked you the best.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, damn you Geller!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Anyway, uhm...Well, I'm glad there's no hard feelings.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: No, none at all. You need to be happy with whoever is in your home... Although if you don't mind telling me, what was your problem? Maybe it's something I can work on in the future.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, you know, it's uhm... nothing you did, it's... it's uhm... my issue.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: What is it...? Please...?
lines.stage_direction: Ross hesitates he tilts his head

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know, I'm just not uhm... that comfortable with a guy who's as sensitive as you.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: That's fair... Although, can I ask... why do you think that is?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why... I... I don't know. Uhm... errrr... maybe... maybe because of my father?

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: hmmm...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean, uhm... you know when I was growing up he was kind of a tough guy... You know a-a-and as a kid I wasn't the athlete I am now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh ha ha!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I play squash...! Anyway, uhm... I uhm... I always get the feeling he thought I was too sensitive.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: That must have been hard.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It was hard... I remember... I was in my bedroom... playing with my dinosaurs... playing and learning ... and my father walks in and says... he says... "What are you doing with those things? What's wrong with you, why aren't you... why aren't you outside playing like a... like a real boy?

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: But you are a real boy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know I am! ...And when it's summer, and it's hot, why can't you wear a tank top?

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: It's allright! Crying is good. It lets the boo-hoos out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here come some more...

lines.text: And what's the one kind of boat that can never, ever sink?

lines.text: What kind?

lines.speaker: Sandy/Grumpus

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! You blow my mind...

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Oh, I gotta go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aaahh... How much do I owe you?

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Twenty bucks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's like the cheapest college ever.

title: The One With Ross's Inappropriate Song

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And that's why, no matter what mommy says, we really were on a break. Yes we were! Yes we were! Come here gorgeous. Oh! Look at you! You are the cutest little baby ever! You're just a... a little bitty baby, you know that? But you've got... You've got big beautiful eyes... Yes you do... and a... and a big round belly. Big baby butt! I like big butts. I like big butts and I cannot lie / you other brothers can't deny / when a girl walks in with an itty, bitty, waist / and a round thing in your face you get ... Oh my God, Emma... you're laughing! Oh my God, you've never done that before, have you? You never done that before... Daddy made you laugh, huh? Well, daddy and Sir Mix Alot... What? What? You... you wanna hear some more? Uhm... My anaconda don't want none / unless you got buns hon ... I'm a terrible father!

scenes.location: Central Perk. Joey sits at a table and Chandler and Monica enter

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey... hey listen... What do you guys know about investments?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I'm starting to make good money on the show and I'm thinking... I should probably do something with it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What do you do with your money now?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I just tape it to the back of my toilet tank. I didn't say that! It's in a bank guarded by robots!
lines.stage_direction: realises that anyone could have overheard that

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you have any ideas?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, yeah... This guy at work got me excited about going in on an emu farm. That'd be kinda cool huh? Pitchin' in on the weekends, helping to plant the emus...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joe... Emus are birds. You raise them for meat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Right! People eat birds... Bird meat... Now do they just fly into your mouth or you go to... you

lines.speaker: go to a restaurant and you say
lines.text: "Excuse me, I'll have a bucket of fried bird." Or... or maybe just a wing or...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, I think you should consider something a little less risky. I mean, I think in this market, real estate is your best investment.The Fed. just lowered the rates and the interest on your mortgage is totally deductible. That's right, I know some stuff!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Real estate, huh? Hmmm...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, and you know who's selling a great apartment? Richard!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, and you know whose knowledge of her ex-boyfriend is shocking? Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My dad told me. They play golf together.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, well... Maybe I'll join them some time. I just hope the club doesn't slip out of my hand and beat the moustache off his face.

lines.text: Hi!

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: Wow, the boyfriend's parents! That's a big step.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really? That hadn't occurred to me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They just gonna love you, just be yourself.

lines.text: Oh yeah, she can't be herself.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, so... allright... Which dress? You can say "neither".

lines.speaker: Rachel and Monica
lines.text: Oh God, neither!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry honey, but we're gonna take you shopping. It's gonna be fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, totally! You are in such good hands. And I'm so good with meeting parents. With the father, you know, you want to flirt a

lines.speaker: little bit, but not in a gross way. Just kind of like
lines.text: "Oh mr. Pincer, I can see where Wallis gets his good looks..."

lines.text: Oh, with the mother, just... just constantly tell her how amazing her son is. Take it from me, moms love me. Ross's mom one time actually said I'm like the daughter that she never had.

lines.speaker: Phoebe

scenes.location: Ross and Rachel's apartment. Rachel enters the apartment

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just finished getting Phoebe all dressed to meet Mike's parents. She's so nervous, it's so sweet!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Guess what? I made Emma laugh today.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You WHAT? And I missed it? Because I was giving a makeover to that stupid hippie?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, and it was uhm... it was like a real little person laugh too. It was... it was like uhm... Only... only not creepy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well... well, what did you do to make her laugh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I uhm... Well, I sang... well actually I rapped... Baby Got Back...
lines.stage_direction: Rachel gasps Rachel's face changes from excited to angry

lines.text: You WHAT? You sang... to our baby daughter... a song about a guy who likes to have sex with women with giant asses?

lines.text: But you know what, if you think about it, it actually promotes a healthy uhm... body image... because... even big butts or uhm... juicy doubles.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: owwwww...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Please don't take her away from me!

scenes.location: Richard's apartment. There's a knock on the door.The listing agent opens the door for Chandler and Joey

lines.speaker: Catherine
lines.text: Oh hi, come on in. I'm Catherine, the listing agent.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi I'm Joey. This is Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So how come Richard's selling the place? Went bankrupt? Medical malpractice? Choked on his own moustache?

lines.speaker: Catherine
lines.text: Actually, he is buying a much bigger place. It's got a great view of Central Pa.....

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: mmm That's enough about you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is there anything we should know about the apartment?

lines.speaker: Catherine
lines.text: All the appliances are included. There is a lot of light, a new kitchen... I think you guys would be very happy here...
lines.stage_direction: Joey and Chandler both realise what she's assuming and start laughing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, no, no, no, NO! No, no... we're not together. We're not a couple. We're definately not a couple.

lines.speaker: Catherine
lines.text: Oh... Okay, sorry!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, you seem pretty insulted by that. What? I'm not good enough for you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We're not gonna have this conversation again... Look at this place. Why am I so intimidated by this guy? Pretentious art, this huge macho couch. When we know all he does is sit around all day crying about losing Monica to a real man! You don't think he's here, do you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You know what it is? It's a nice place but I gotta see I don't know if I see myself living here. Oh, oh, oh, let me see... Yeah, I could see it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look at these videos. You know, I mean, who does he think he is? Magnum Force, Dirty Harry, Cool Hand Luke... Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There's a tape here with Monica's name on it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh! A tape with a girls name on it. It's probably a sex tape... Wait a minute... This says Monica... And this is Richard's apartment...

lines.text: Get there faster!

scenes.location: Mike's parents building. Phoebe gets out of the classy elevator

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Wow! You look like... like my mom.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm wearing pantyhose!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Great! Come on in!
lines.stage_direction: Mike kisses her on the cheek. A butler walks in and takes Phoebe's coat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, thank you! Oh... Oh my God, you're RICH!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No, my parents are rich.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, so... They gotta die someday. HELLO!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Mom, dad, this is Phoebe. Phoebe, these are my parents: Theodore and Bitsy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Theodore... Bitsy... What a delight!

lines.speaker: Bitsy
lines.text: It's so nice to finally meet you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And you... Your home is lovely.

lines.speaker: Bitsy
lines.text: Well thank you, I'll give you a tour later. It's actually three floors.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Holy crap!

lines.speaker: Bitsy
lines.text: Phoebe, why don't you come in the living room and meet our friends?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, try and stop me!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hey... Wh... What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm trying to get your parents to like me.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah, I'm sure they will, but you don't have to do this... I'm wanting them to get to know Phoebe, not Phoebe...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Got it! It... It's hard to stop...

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well, come on...

lines.speaker: Theodore
lines.text: Phoebe, these are our friends, Tom and Sue Angle.

lines.speaker: Bitsy
lines.text: Phoebe, come sit. Tell us a little bit about yourself... So where are you from?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uhm... Okay, well, allright, uhm... Originally I'm from upstate, but uhm... then my mom killed herself and my stepdad went to prison, so... I just moved to the city where uhm... I actually lived in a burned out Buick LeSabre for a while... which was okay, that was okay, until uhm... I got hepatitis, you know, 'cause this pimp spit in my mouth and... but I... I got over it and uhm... anyway, now I'm uhm... a freelance massage therapist, uhm... which, you know, isn't always steady money but at least I don't pay taxes, huh... So... where does everyone summer?
lines.stage_direction: no accent frowns are received everyone in the room finds it a bit surreal, which Phoebe realises and starts to talk in the accent again

scenes.scene_description: Mike's parents building. Phoebe gets out of the classy elevator, looking all dressed up like an older woman, and very un-Phoebe. She walks to the door and rings the doorbell.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: God! God! This is not going well.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No, no, no, you're doing fine, really... Why don't you go talk to my dad?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, okay, okay, okay... Still sure about me being myself?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Absolutely! Or maybe just a little less pimp spit.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So Theodore... I uhm... I can see where Mike gets his good looks from...

lines.speaker: Theodore
lines.text: Oh... Well...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah... And that physique! You must work out all the time...

lines.speaker: Theodore
lines.text: Oh no, not all the time... I do the best I can...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah I bet! Look out!
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe punches Theodore right in the stomach

lines.speaker: Theodore
lines.text: OH! OWWWWW!
lines.stage_direction: Theodore grabs his stomach in agony

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Theodore
lines.text: I recently had surgery.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm so sorry!

lines.speaker: Theodore
lines.text: No, I'll be fine... I just should check the stitches...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I really, really am sorry.

lines.speaker: Theodore
lines.text: How could you know. Why wouldn't you punch me in the stomach?
lines.stage_direction: Theodore walks out... Mike walks towards Phoebe

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Uhm... Did you just hit my dad?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes... I'm sorry, I've never met a boyfriends parents before...

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: But, I mean, you have met... humans before, right? Look, why don't you go talk to my mom?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah okay... yeah, your mom... okay... She looks nice, I can talk to her.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah, you do that, and I go check my dad for signs of internal bleeding.
lines.stage_direction: Mike walks away and Bitsy walks in the same direction.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah... Oh Bitsy, hi. Uhm... listen I just wanted to thank you again for having me here tonight.

lines.speaker: Bitsy
lines.text: Well, not at all...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Also uhm... I just want you to know what a wonderful man your son is.

lines.speaker: Bitsy
lines.text: Thank you, I think so too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, and you know, it really is a testament to how he was raised. Especially to you. Because he's very respectful of women.

lines.speaker: Bitsy
lines.text: Is he really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you kidding. He is so considerate of my feelings and... you know I think... you'd also like to know that he is a very gentle lover.

lines.speaker: Bitsy
lines.text: E-e-excuse me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, no, no, no, no, no. Don't get me wrong. No, he's not in like a sissy way. No, no, no... when he gets going, he can rattle a headboard like a sailor on leave...
lines.stage_direction: Mike now enters and stands behind Phoebe

lines.speaker: Bitsy
lines.text: That's... my boy.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Awesome!

scenes.location: Monica and Chandler's apartment. Only Chandler is there with the videotape in his hands

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not gonna watch it... I don't NEED to watch it... I mean, what good could possibly come from watching? Well, we know I'm gonna watch it.

lines.text: Hey dude, what's up?

lines.text: Don't judge me, I'm only human!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Did you take that tape?

lines.text: I had to! Okay, imagine you were married... and you found a tape of your wife in another guys' apartment... Wouldn't you need to know what was on it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know. Who'm I'm married to?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Some girl...!?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She hot?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah...!?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How did she get me to settle down?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Allright, I'm gonna watch it... I mean look, it's probably not even what I think it is... And even if it is... It can't possibly be as bad as what I'm picturing in my head... Can it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Guess I don't know. My experience: if a girl says yes to being taped... She doesn't say no to much else, I tell ya...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Then you're gonna have to watch it for me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? Whoo... What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Just for a few seconds, so I can know what it is... Please?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, fine... But if I enjoy this, you have only yourself to blame...
lines.stage_direction: Chandler turns his back to the TV. Joey puts the tape in the VCR, switches it on and watches what's on the tape... It's clearly a american football match, with the referee's whistle blowing, the crowds cheering...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why am I hearing cheering?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well it's okay, its like... its just a football game.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Football? Just football?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, see... you were all worried for nothing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's football... It's just football... This is great! This is the first time I've ever enjoyed football... It may be customary to get a beer... What the... What are you doing?
lines.stage_direction: Chandler walks to the fridge, his back turned to the TV and a moaning sound replaces the cheering of the crowd... Joey's eyes double in size... Chandler turns around, but Joey already took a sprint for Chandler, jumps, and floors Chandler in the open space in front of the apartment door...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You don't wanna see what I just saw!
lines.stage_direction: at this moment Monica comes home, and sees Chandler flat on his back on the floor and Joey pinning him down

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you guys doing? Oh my God, is that Richard?
lines.stage_direction: Monica hears the moaning coming from the TV and looks at it It only takes a split second for Joey to realise, he pulls Monica down by her jacket, and she falls, face down next to Chandler. Chandler gets up a bit, and Joey quickly covers Chandler's eyes with his hand.
