lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no I need a good lie to explain why I wasn't at a work thing today.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, honey. You stink at lying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I do not.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh really. Okay. let me ask you something. Yesterday at the coffee house, I went to the bathroom and when I came back, my muffin was gone-who took it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Somebody opened the door to the coffee house and a raccoon came running in, went straight for your muffin and I said "Hey don't eat that-that's Phoebe's" and he said.. <pause> He said.. "Joey you stink at lying." What am I going to do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don't worry, don't worry. We'll come up with a good lie. I'll help you practice it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh great, that'd be great. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure, what.. what was the work thing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh.. <forgetting what the work thing was, rolls up his sleeve on his right arm and shows Phoebe, she looks>

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: "Pick up grandma at the airport"?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh.. man..

lines.speaker: Amy with straight hair
lines.text: Oh she's precious. Do you ever worry she's going to get your real nose?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Amy! <pause> Yes I do.. I really do. <grabs Ross' hand for support>

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Hello? Yeah, um. Hang on one sec. <to Ross and Rachel> Can I take this upstairs?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure, we don't live there but...

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Seriously? Its.. its just these rooms? <moves hands around motioning 'just these rooms'> <To Ross> I thought you were a doctor.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, no. Ross has a PhD.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Ew. <walks into Rachel's room, I'm guessing>

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: God she is unbelievable.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know, I mean a PhD is just as good as an MD.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh sure Ross, yeah. If I have a heart attack in a restaurant, I want you there with your fossil brush.

lines.speaker: Amy storms out
lines.text: Stupid Thanksgiving.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? What happened?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: My boyfriend canceled on me. I mean.. I I finally find a real relationship. I mean, someone that I can spend this day with and then his wife comes back into town. I swear, its almost not worth dating married guys.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Don't say that.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh. I was so looking forward to this. It was going to be such a beautiful Thanksgiving. We were going to have sushi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Amy, don't cry Amy. Um.. Ross, could I talk to you in private?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure, you want to go upstairs?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um look I was thinking.. If its ok with Monica I would like to invite Amy to Thanksgiving.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know, I think thats a great idea. It'll be like the pilgrims bringing the Indians syphilis.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look I know she's a little tough to take. She has no where else to go, and she's my sister. Alright, she's Emma's aunt. And I would like them to bond.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, fine, but I don't want them bonding to much. I don't want her telling Emma she needs a nose job.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, you know what? She may need one..We're just going to have to make our peace with that!

lines.speaker: Rachel opens their door
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Everyone there says
lines.text: Hi, hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey you guys, this is my sister Amy. This is Chandler, Joey, Phoebe and you know Mon.

lines.speaker: Everyone
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh my god. You're on Days of Our Lives.

lines.speaker: Joey laughs
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: wow. They must put a lot of makeup on you.

lines.speaker: Joey looks rejected
lines.text: Hap.. Hap.. Happy Thanksgiving.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica to Emma
lines.text: Hey you.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: So. Welcome. Is this your first time you're seeing Emma?

lines.speaker: Amy with confused look on face
lines.text: Yeah I.. I think so. <sticks her hand out to shake hands with Phoebe and says to her> It's nice to meet you Emma.

lines.speaker: Phoebe shakes her hand and says
lines.text: Phoe-Be.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh. That's a funny noise.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs, I still need some help here

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh right, ok um. Ok so its not just the lie you tell. but its the way you tell it. . For example if you look down at the ground when you're talking, people know you're lying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh.. I don't know why this is so hard for me. you know.. I mean lying is basically just acting and I am a terrfic actor.

lines.speaker: Phoebe looks down
lines.text: You are a terrific actor.

lines.speaker: Amy coming out of the bathroom
lines.text: Hey. Hey where's the baby?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh we just put her down for a nap.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh I was just thinking. You know what would be incredible? If you guys died.

lines.speaker: Ross first has a look of 'huh' then changes it to sarcastic happy
lines.text: Thank you Amy.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: no, no, then I would get the baby. I mean you know it would be just like a movie. Like at first I wouldn't know what to do with her, then I would rise to the occasion and and then I would get a makeover and then I'd get married.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thats a great movie. <she claps>

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Now listen, not that you guys could stop me or anything cause you know you'd be dead. I was thinking about changing her name. I'm just not really a big fan of Emily. [Transcriber note: I'm surprised that Rachel and Ross didn't say they weren't either here]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Emma.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Emma, Ross wants you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: PHOE-BE.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why does she keep making that noise?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, I don't know how to tell you this, but um, if something were to happen to Ross or to myself <Ross and Rachel knock on wood> um you wouldn't get the baby.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Well who would?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well we haven't offically asked them yet, but we would want Monica and Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe you'd want us to raise Emma.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: yeah oh my god, I'm so moved.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: I don't believe this, hold on a second. You guys die and I don't get your baby?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: See look Amy, we're a lot closer to Monica and Chandler. We see them every day. And truthfully honey, you don't seem very connected to the baby.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Connected? I mean.. to what? She's.. she's a lump.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, guys I got to say. This means so much to me. That you would trust me with your child. I mean, we all know that Monica and I have been trying to have a baby of our own. You know I've had my doubts about my skills as a father, but that you two.. that you two.... <starts to cry>

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: <points to Chandler> This guy? Seriously?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! It's time for dinner. Everyone we're using our fancy china.. um and its very expensive so please be careful.

lines.speaker: Ross starts playing with a plate
lines.text: Woah. Woah... <nearly really drops the plate and more seriously> Woah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, just to be clear comedy with the plates will not be well recieved. <pinches Ross' arm>

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! How come my plate's less fancy then everyone else's? Do you not trust me with a fancy plate?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, honey, its. thats a special plate. See its a game, whoever gets that plate wins.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't believe I won.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Its such a slap in the face. I'm your sister and you would give your baby to these strangers over me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica is Ross' sister.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: No, Ross' sister was really fat.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That was me.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: No, she was this really dorky girl in high school that used to follow Rachel around like a puppy dog.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey. Amy. You've got to stop doing that.

lines.speaker: Monica about to have a heart attack
lines.text: Okay, listen I know you're having a little bit of a family crisis, but you don't have to take it out on the plates. I mean, I mean in fact I think that everyone should cut their food like this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now see, this way you protect the plate.. and lets face it you have fun.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Okay, how about this, you guys die and the crazy plate lady dies, then do I get the baby?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, if crazy plate lad.. <sees Monica frustrated at this comment> If Monica dies then I would get Emma, Right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well actually...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Actually what?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: well.. its just. its just in that case, then um. Emma would go to my parents.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Hurts, doesn't it?

lines.speaker: Joey raises his hand
lines.text: Uh.. who has to die for me to get her?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So if Monica's not around, then I'm not good enough to raise Emma?

lines.speaker: Ross looks down
lines.text: No, that that is not what we're saying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah he's lying. He looked down.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well what is wrong with me? Am I .. am I incomptent? Because I managed to survive whatever it is that killed the three of you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, you're taking this the wrong way. We think you're going to be a wonderful parent. It's just.. you're more the fun parent.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah and we'd want to make sure Emma has someone like Monica who is more uh. uh discliplinarin.. someone who can be firm and strict.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thats not how you see me, is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe while cutting a sweet potatoe in the air
lines.text: No you're all about the fun.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I may not know a lot about babies, but do you really think I'm not capable?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, you both are equally capable. Its just.. you're strongest when.. when you're together.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok. So if we both had Emma and I die <knocks on table> she'd have to give her up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure, Monica would have to give her up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I lie better then that, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So.. let me get this straight. So my two friends die, I get Emma. Then my wife dies, then Emma the one tiny ray of hope left in my life gets taken away from me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There's your movie! <claps>

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey. There you are. You disappeared after dinner.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh? Did somebody miss me? Is there a child to raise poorly?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross and Rachel don't know what they're talking about. I mean its not like their so responsible. Emma is a product of a bottle of Merlot and a five year old condom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah but they're right. I mean, I'm not a strong father figure and I never will be.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No you learn these things. You grow into it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, but its not who I am. Everything they said was exaclty why I was worried about having a kid. And its true. And look everybody knows it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know it! I want to have a kid with you because I think you're going to be an amazing dad... at the fun parts and the hard parts.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, well can you picture me saying "Go to your room! You're grounded"?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Can you hear me say "You're grounded"?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You said that to me last week.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How hard is it? No shoes on the furniture.

lines.speaker: Joey groans and gets off the phone
lines.text: The producer from Days left a message on my machine asking why I wasn't at the parade. They said everybody's pissed off at me.. <whiny voice> And they all got to meet Santa!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's ok. I thought of the perfect lie for you. It's easy to remember and doesn't invite a lot of questions. You weren't at the parade because you had a family emergency.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I like that, yeah. Wasn't at the parade because I had a family emergency.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, what happened?

lines.speaker: Joey all nervous and looking down and fiddling with his ear
lines.text: Oh.. My sister's raccoon.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! Nothing with a raccoon.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Arg... Alright, I'll take care of it. <throws hands out in the air>

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh wait.. What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Setting the table.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah we thought it would be nice to use the fancy china for dessert too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh how nice. Maybe later we can all go blow our noses on my wedding dress.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey dude, you okay? Sorry about before.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no thats okay, you're totally right. I don't know anything about disciplining a child. But it did hurt my feelings and I want you to know that when I die, you don't get Joey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ucch. <pauses> Uchh <louder this time> In case you hadn't noticed, I'm not talking to you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: UCHH! <much louder and longer then Amy's>

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You know.. this.. this is classic Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.. yeah right.. Remember in high school when I died and didn't give you my baby?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: This might be my one chance to have a baby Rachel. I mean, you know that I have been so busy focusing on my carrer.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? What carrer?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Um.. I'm a decorator.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok. You decorate dad's office and so now you're a decorator. Okay! I went to the zoo yesterday and now I'm a koala bear.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Why can't you ever be supportive?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sup.. You want to talk supportive? You didn't even come and visit me when I was in the hospital having the baby.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh. Yeah. Well.. You didn't come see me when I was in the hospital when I was getting my lips done.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I did the first time! Oh. Oh.. <gets up and walks into the kitchen> And you know what. You want to know why I'm not giving Emily to you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Emma.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh whose side are you on? <back to Amy> I'm not giving you Emma because there is no way you could handle the responibility of a child.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: How hard could it be? You do it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: ooh oooh..

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Do you want to know why you don't want me to have the baby?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: uh huh.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Because you don't want me to be happy. You.. you have always been jealous of me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Jealous of what? Of your lack of responsiblity? You, your immaturity? Your total disregard of other people's feelings?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Uh.. To name a few. You know.. You know.. You've just always been like this. You just have to have everything. And I couldn't have anything. Like in junior high, when you stole Timmy from me. I mean, do you even realize how much that hurt me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Timmy was my boyfriend and you made out with him!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Oh come on, that was 20 years ago. Get over it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I cannot, I cannot believe that I invited you here today.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Yeah, well you know what I cannot believe. That my so-called sister, gets a 30% discount from Ralph Lauren and I still have to pay retail.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ah ha ha. ah ha ha. <evil meancing laughter> It's forty five.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: You bitch. You just think you're so perfect. With your new baby and your, your small apartment. <directs this to Ross who in turns throws the towel in his hand down on the table> Well let me tell you something. Your baby isn't even that cute.

lines.speaker: Ross walks over
lines.text: Too far, Amy. Too far.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You take that back.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Take it back!

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: No! What are you going to do? Make me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Heey man, I work out.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: So do I.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I do pilates.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: I do yoga.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Bring it on!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Put the plates in the boxes!! Put the plates in the boxes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Did you just push me?

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: Uh, yeah I think I did.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright. Thats it! <Pushes Amy back>

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Forget the bubblewrap! There isn't time!

lines.speaker: Rachel starts messing up Amy's hair
lines.text: Frizzy frizzy frizzy frizzy!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my god! Shouldn't we stop this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? Are you out of your mind? Lets throw some jello on them.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ew! Gross.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright! That is it. This is our apartment and you can not behave this way. Now if you can't act your age then you shouldn't be here at all. Now those plates may not be as nice as the pretty pink ones I picked out, but they're very important to Monica. I want you to apologize to her right now.

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mon, I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Thats better. Now I want you to both apologize to each other and mean it.

lines.speaker: Amy and Rachel at same time
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: By the way, that fight was totally arousing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dude. Well done. You know what? If I die, and Rachel dies and Monica dies then you can totally take care of Emma.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah? Well thanks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, so now do I get Joey?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, but you should know he eats five times a day and shoves pennies up his nose.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you okay Mon?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: uh huh.. I mean these things happen. Its' just a plate. Its not like somebody died.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's all right. You can mourn.

lines.speaker: Monica starts crying
lines.text: Thank you. It was so beautiful. <gets up and walks towards the front door> I'm going to go to Joeys and get the pies.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Actually its not pies, its just pie.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't care. <pauses and realizes...> Oh my god. I've lost the will to scold.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look Amy, it got a little of control..Um.. and I'm sorry. You're my sister and uh.. if it really means that much to you..

lines.speaker: Amy
lines.text: So you're going to give me the baby?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh. No.. I was going to let you use my Ralph Lauren discount.

lines.speaker: Amy starting to cry
lines.text: You are not going to regret this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She needs changing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no no no.. I'll get her. I'm super-compentent and totally responsibile and fourth in line to raise Emma. I'll be right there Emma. Just let me get my trusty diaper bag here. <knocks over the box of china> Well.. what do you know? I guess, I'll be the one who dies first.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandlers apartment.

scenes.location: Chandler is packing the broken china in its box. He's taping up the top of box so thoroughly

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, does Monica know about her broken plates yet?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nope...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Broke them all, huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yep...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You gonna tell her?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nope... Hey... so I'm gonna... put the plates back. You know, I think you were right, I don't think we should use these plates again for a looong time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Like only if the queen comes?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Maybe not even then.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! I did it. I called my producer. I told him I had a family emergency, he totally bought it. Thanks for teaching me how to lie Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No problem! Next week: stealing...
lines.stage_direction: Chandler walks away to store the box of broken china.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bye plates!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, you told her you broke all the plates, huh?
lines.stage_direction: Chandler walks back, looking angrily at Joey

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? Something happened with the plates?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhm... Yeah... this uhm... raccoon came in...

scenes.scene_description: Chandler is packing the broken china in its box. He's taping up the top of box so thoroughly, there isn't an inch of cardboard which isn't covered in tape. He is struggling with the tape dispenser.

title: FRIENDS

lines.speaker: Directed by
lines.text: Ben Weiss

lines.speaker: Transcript by
lines.text: David Buehrle

scenes.location: Chandler's office in Tulsa.�� His assistant is showing him photographs

lines.speaker: JO LYNN
lines.text: This kitty is Mittens and this one is Fitzhugh, and this little guy in the cat condo is Jinkies.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yep.� That's a lot of cats Jo Lynn.� Single are ya?

scenes.scene_description: Chandler's office in Tulsa.�� His assistant is showing him photographs.

scenes.location: Chandler's office in Tulsa.�� His assistant is showing him photographs

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Chandler Bing.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.� How come you're answering your own phone?� Where's your crazy assistant?

scenes.scene_description: Phone rings.� Chandler answers using the speaker phone.

scenes.location: Chandler's office in Tulsa.�� His assistant is showing him photographs

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: What's up Joe?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Okay, what have we always wanted to do together?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Braid each other's hair and ride horseback on the beach?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No, no, no.� When you get home tomorrow night, you and I are going to be at the Wizzards-Knicks game . . .� courtside!

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Courtside?� Oh my God.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah.� Maybe Michael Jordon will dive for the ball and break my jaw with his knee.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: That's so cool.� I'll let Monica know.

scenes.scene_description: She frowns.� Chandler picks up the hand set.

scenes.location: Chandler's office in Tulsa.�� His assistant is showing him photographs

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Joey just called.� He's got courtside Knicks tickets for him and me tomorrow night.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Really?� But tomorrow night is the only night I get off from the restaurant.� If you go to the game, we won't have a night together for another week.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: But hey, it's courtside.� The cheerleaders are going to be right in fr. . . � That's not the way to convince you.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Chandler look, I don't want to be one of those wives who says, "You can't go to the game.� You have to spend time with me."� So, if you could just realize it on your own . . .

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I know.� You're right.� I want to see you too.� I've just got to figure out a way to tell Joey, you know?� He's really looking forward to this.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Tell him that you haven't seen your wife in a long time.� Tell him that having a long-distance relationship is really difficult.� Tell him that what little time we have is precious.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, ah, ah . . .� I'll think of something.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler hangs up and calls Monica who is reading a book on their sofa as the phone rings.

scenes.location: Ross and Rachel's apartment.� Ross is in the living room covering Emma.� Rachel enters wearing a sexy dress

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Wow!� � Wow, You look . . . uh . . .�� It's just, ah . . .�� That dress . . . uh . . .�

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well, I hope the ends of these sentences are good.

lines.text: I know. They should be a family. They should get married and have more children.

lines.text: Yuck! But we should probably keep it down; she's still in the bedroom.

lines.text: So, what are we gonna do? Are we just gonna go ahead and set them up with people?

lines.text: Ooh, that's a great plan!

lines.text: Guys, guys, I've got great news! Guess what… Joey : Uh, ah, Monica's pregnant?! Monica : Really? Let's get past the moment. Phoebe : What's your news? Chandler : Thank you. I got a job in advertising. Monica : Oh, honey, that's incredible! Phoebe : Gosh, what's the pay like? Oh, come on people… come on, now, if I don't know who makes the most, how do I know who I like the most! Hey Joey! Chandler : Actually, it pays nothing. It's an internship. Joey : Oh, that's cool. We have interns at 'Days Of Our Lives'. Chandler : Right. So, it'll be the same except… less sex with you. Ross : So, uh, what kinda stuff do you think they'll have you do there? I should have written it down!

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: This is Joey Tribbiani. Joey, these are the producers and, as you probably already know, this is Lennart Haze. Joey : It is so amazing to meet you. I'm such a big fan of your work. Lennart : Well, I've… I've been blessed with a… a lot of great roles. Joey : Tell me about it! "Unlimited nights and weekends!" Lennart : You making fun of me? Because I am not a sell-out. I didn't do that for the money, I believe in those phones. I almost lost a cousin because of bad wireless service. Joey : No, I-I-I wasn't making fun of you, honestly, I-I think you were great in those commercials. Lennart : Really? Joey : Yeah. Lennart : Well, I do bring a certain credibility to the role. Joey : Are you kiddin'? When they shoot you out of that cannon… Lennart : Peeeeeooooooooch "Hang up that phone!" One take! Joey : Wow! Lennart : So, shall we read? Joey : Oh, yah, sure. Lennart : Top of act two. This is my entrance. You got it? Lennart : "What the hell are you still doing here"? Joey : Err, "I think you know". Lennart : "Bastard"! Joey : "I am what you made me. You know what? I could go right now." Lennart : "Go, go!" Joey : "I can't. Oh, I want to, long pause, but I can't." Lennart : I'm sorry, sorry. You're not supposed to say "long pause" Joey : Oh, oh, I thought that was your character's name, you know, I thought you were like an Indian or something, you know with a… Lennart : No. Thank you so much for coming in. We appreciate it, thank you. Joey : Ah, y-y-you're sure you don't want me to do it again? I could do it with an accent, you know, Southern "I could go right now, maaan!" Lennart : My god in heaven. Producer #1 : Joey, hang on for a second. Lennart, can we talk to you for a moment? Lennart : You, you gotta be kidding. See, h-he, he can't act. . Lennart : Hey! I-I-I don't care if he's hot, you know. If you want to sleep with him, do it on your own time. This is a play. No, listen: if you insist on this, I will call my agent so fast on a cell phone that has a connection that is so clear he's gonna think I'm next door. Joey : Ah, hi, ah. Thank you so much for whispering for my benefit, but, ah, look, if you just tell me what I did wrong, I'd just love to work on it and come back and try it again for you. And, and also: 'How you doing?' You should, please, just gimme another chance. I really wanna get better, please. Lennart : Well, if you wanna come back at the end of the day today, here are my notes. Ready? Joey : Yeah. Lennart : Uhm, you're in your head. You-you're thinking way too much. Joey : I really doubt that. Lennart : No, no, no. It's that you're not connected with anything in your body. There's no urgency. The scene is a struggle, uhm, it's a race. Also, what you did was horizontal. Don't be afraid to explore the vertical. And don't learn the words. Let the words learn you. Joey : Couldn't I just sleep with the producer?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, how did the audition go? Joey : Well, they wanna see me again this afternoon, but, err, well, Lennart Haze did not like me. Monica : What happened? Joey : Well, he said I wasn't urgent enough, you know, and that everything I did was horizontal and I should be more vertical. Oh, and he said that I should think less. Monica : So far so good! Chandler : Honey, I'm old! Monica : What's wrong? Chandler : I am so much older than these other interns. I can't compete with them. Monica : So you're a little older. Try to look at the positive: You have all this life experience. Chandler : Yes, but I don't think life experience with these. Joey : Wooooooooow It's like they're on fire! Monica : What are they? Chandler : They're these prototype sneakers and come up with ideas on how to sell them which I can't do because no self-respecting adult would ever where these. Joey : I give you $ 500 for them! Chandler : What am I supposed to do with these? Monica : Ah, come on, sneakers are easy. You wear sneakers all the time. Chandler : Well, first of all, they're not called "sneakers" anymore. Apparently, they're called "kicks" or "skids" and I think I heard somebody say "slorps". And here, look: they've got these wheels to pop out from the bottom so you can roll around 'cause, apparently, walking is too much exercise. Kids, kids, roll your way to childhood obesity! Would you help me try to sell these? Monica : Okay, have you considered using a girl with huge knockers? Chandler : No, I don't think that's the kinda thing they're looking for. Joey : Hey, that'd work on me! Why did I get to buy Mrs. Butterwords?
lines.stage_direction: He sits down Joey nods entering, carrying a large box standing up, walking towards him He opens the box and takes an inline skate-like sneaker out He takes the sneaker They sit down again determined He takes a sneaker to Monica

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey, hey! Ross : Hey, you'll never guess what just happened… Phoebe and I got mugged! Monica : You okay?! Ross : Yah, because Phoebe knew the mugger! Monica : How do you know a mugger? Phoebe : I'm sorry I have friends outside the six of us. Ross : You wanna know how she knew him? Because Phoebe used to mug people. Monica : Seriously? Phoebe : Well, I'm not proud of it, but, you know… sometimes when I was living on the street and I needed money for food and stuff I… Monica : Phoebe, that is awful! Phoebe : Well, ok, I wasn't rich like you guys, ok, I didn't eat gold and have a flying pony… I had a hard life, my mother was killed by a drug dealer… Monica : You're mother killed herself! Phoebe : She was a drug dealer! Ross : Well, anyway, it was a good thing Phoebe knew the knew him, because I was about to do some serious damage! Phoebe : Okay… Monica : Well, this must've brought back some really bad memories for you, Ross. Phoebe : Why? Monica : Well, Ross was mugged as a kid. Phoebe : You were? Ross : Yeah, it was pretty traumatic. I was outside St. Marc's Comics… you know, I-I-I was just there minding my own business, you know, seeing what kinda trouble spiderman got into that week- Monica : Wonderwoman! Ross : Anyway, I was heading towards this bakery, you know, to pick up a couple of dozen Linzer torts for someone… … when outta nowhere this thug with a pipe jumps out and says: "Gimme your money, punk!" Phoebe : Oh my god… Ross : I know! And-and the worst part was they took my backpack which had all the original artwork I had done for my own comic book: "Science Boy" Monica : Oh yeah! What was his superpower again? Ross : A superhuman thirst for knowledge. Monica : That's it. Ross : Well, I-I better get to class Are there any more of your friends I should look out for on my way, Phoebe? Phoebe : No… actually, you might wanna stay away from Jane street… that's where stabby Joe works.
lines.stage_direction: sitting down bewildered He points at Phoebe shocked disapproving menacingly laughing worried coughing He looks at Monica shocked He stands up

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.text: You're late! Joey : I know, I'm sorry, but can I just have a quick second to run to the bathroom? Receptionist : No, Lennart doesn't wait! Joey : But I'm bursting with u-hu! Lennart : Joey! Here we go. Let's go very quickly! Joey : Actually, I really need… Lennart : We must go now, quickly, please. Joey : Yes… ahahaaa Lennart : Ready? "What the hell are you still doing here?" Joey : "I think you know!" Lennart : "Oh, you sick bastard!" Joey : "I am what you made me! You know what?" Lennart : "What?" Joey : "I could go right now." Lennart : "Then go, go!" Joey : "I, oh, I can't. I want to, but I can't!" Lennart : Cut! That was good. That was very good. You did everything I asked for. Joey : I did? Lennart : Yes. Plus… what you've got that… I don't know what you've got going… this squirmy quality that you bring into the character that I couldn't've even imagined. Wow, hey, here's what we gonna do: come back tomorrow for the final callbacks with the ***, do all of this what you've got going now, but – you know what? – more, more. Can you do that? Joey : Sure, yeah. I don't have time to say thank you because I really gotta go. Lennart : Look at that: still in character, I like it… I plant seeds I can't explain.
lines.stage_direction: entering spotting Joey interrupting He seems to be in pain walking on the spot urgent He turns to the producers disbelieving He grabs his jacket and limps out of the room watching Joey leave

lines.text: Hey! Chandler : Hey! Monica : What are you doing? Chandler : Putting on the sneakers… thought I'd get into a younger mindset, you know, to see if it sparked anything… Rachel : Oh, anything yet? Chandler : Yes, how's this: They're so uncomfortable it's like getting kicked in the nuts for your feet! Joey : Hey! All : Hey! Joey : Probably wanna know what I'm doing…? Monica : No, that seems about right. Joey : Yesterday at my audition, I really had to pee, and apparently, having to pee makes me a really good actor. I got a call-back, so I'm drinking everything. Oh, by the way, that egg nog in our fridge was great! Rachel : Joey, that was formula. Joey : We gotta get more of that. Chandler : You know what… these aren't half-bad! You should suggest something like these to Ralph Lauren. Rachel : Okay, first of all, that's stupid and second of all, I'm not allowed to talk to Ralph. Chandler : Alright I feel youger already! Chandler : Yah, I think I broke my hip.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, you! Ross : Hey! Phoebe : Hey, how was class? Ross : No one ever asked me that, what's wrong? Phoebe : Nothing, I really wanna know. Ross : Oh… Well, uhm, there was actually a rather lively discussion about the Pleistocene… Phoebe : Alright, nothing is worth this. Uhm, I have a confession to make… uhm, okay, you know, that girl that mugged you when you were a kid… Ross : Wh-What are you talking about? It wasn't a girl. It was this huge dude. Phoebe : You don't have to lie anymore, Ross, I know that it was a fourteen year-old girl. Ross : No, it wasn't. Phoebe : Yes, it was. Ross : No, it wasn't. You don't think I would've defended myself against a fourteen year-old… Phoebe : "Gimme your money, punk!" Ross : Oh my god, it was you! I can't believe it, you… you mugged me? Phoebe : Yeah, and I'm so, so sorry, Ross, I'm sorry, but, you know, if you think about it, it's kinda neat. I mean, well, it's just that I I've always felt kinda like an outsider, you know, the rest of you have these connections that go way back and, you know, now, you and I have… have a great one! Ross : It's not the best! Phoebe : I know, I'm sorry, please forgive me. I don't know what to say… Ross : There's nothing you can say. That was the most humiliating thing that ever happened to me. Phoebe : Really? Even more humiliating than… Ross : Hey, let's not do this!

lines.speaker: Scene
lines.text: Chandler and Monica's apartment. Chandler and Monica are looking through some papers.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did you see our bank statement? Can this be right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know...God. I haven't seen my savings take a hit like this since I was a kid and they came up with double-stuffed Oreos. What happened to all our money?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not sure what they did, but I'm inclined to blame Enron.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess with you doing the internship, we're just spending more than we're bringing in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Maybe I should quit and get a job that pays.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, But you're finally doing something that you love! I can't ask you to give that up. Though it'd be nice if the thing that you love was y'know... finding gold.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know what? You know what? I think we're making too big a deal out of this. ok? So we pay our bills a little late this month and maybe next month we cut back on a few things. And maybe we start eating out of Joey's refrigerator for a change. You're chef... what can you make out of backing soda and beer?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok worse case scenario is...we borrow some money from my parents.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! We're not borrowing money.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because we don't do that. We are Bings! And if there's one thing my father taught me was... well to always knock before going into the pool house... but the other thing was never borrow money.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! I Had no idea you had this much pride.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's right! I do! And I'm your man. And I'm going to get us through this situation even if it means you working twice as hard.

lines.text: Wow, Mike Hannigan...You sure know how to make a girl say "Hell yeah!"

lines.text: So we're doing it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Let's do it! Let's live together! Oh god, we're really going to move in together!

lines.text: Yeah!

lines.text: Look, guys, you can't do this, it's just going to make getting over each other, that much harder.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not if nothing happens. Why can't... why can't we just hang out as friends?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sure! If you're just gonna hang out as friends, then maybe I'll join ya. You know, I'm your friend and Mike's friend .

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sit down.

scenes.scene_description: Monica opens the door and Mike is standing at the doorway

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh wow wow wow!! Make room for your friend!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: So how've you been?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I've been pretty good!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You look really beautiful.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thanks, you look good too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no no no no... this is dangerous territory. Keep it clean!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So how's the piano playing going?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Actually I've been playing a lot of love songs lately. I've missed you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I've missed you too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know, on the way over here, I saw this drunk guy throw up. And then a pigeon ate it!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe and Mike sit next to each other

scenes.location: Ross's Apartment: Ross is doing something on his laptop when Chandler walks in

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, Ross, I just wanted to apologize... ..don't tell me you actually made those gay pictures of me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: uhu uhu, check this out.
lines.stage_direction: Chandler sits down and looks at laptop screen

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Huh! So that's what I would look like if I worked out... and was being serviced by a policeman. You're not actually going to send these out are ya?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Eh..actually no, I don't need to because your little "Ross is dead" joke didn't work, ok, there were no responses. Nobody posted anything on the website, nobody called my parents, so the joke my friend is on you. Nobody called, nobody wrote anything, nobody cares that I'm dead. Oh my God! Nobody cares that I'm dead!?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, come on, you know that's not true.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you talking about? You get sixty responses just for coming out of the closet! I didn't get one response! And I'm dead!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, the gay community is a lot more vocal than the dead community.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe this. Not even my geology lab partner? And I carried that guy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright look, let's think about this, ok, do you really think that people are gonna stir up your family at this tragic time? That people are gonna post condolences on a website? This is not about people not caring that you're dead .This is about people not having a decent outlet for their grief.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're right. There isn't a decent outlet.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right, I mean, come on, I'm sure that if you had a funeral or a memorial service, tons of people would come.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Exactly!!
lines.stage_direction: sits back down at his laptop and starts typing

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross, what're you... what're you... what are you doing? You're having a memorial service for yourself!?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! That would be stupid! You're having it for me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross, don't press send, don't press se... !

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: oh, too late, too late! It's sent... oops sorry and so is the picture of you and the police man...

scenes.scene_description: Ross's Apartment: Ross is doing something on his laptop when Chandler walks in

scenes.location: Joey's room: Joey is sitting on his bed reading DooL transcript when Rachel walks in

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm trying to put Emma down for a nap, have you seen Hugsy?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Original or crappy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Original.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, sorry haven't seen him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Then what's that big lump under your covers?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's Monica, ok?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's not Monica!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright!! Fine! It's original Hugsy! No, now I know that Emma wants him but he's mine and I need him..

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: ... she's being unreasonable!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, there is a reason that Emma loves that stupid penguin so much Oh don't cover its ears! It's because it reminds her of her uncle Joey!
lines.stage_direction: yelling Joey covers Hugsy's ears stops yelling

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It does?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! And she's comforted by him because she loves her uncle Joey so much.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? She... she loves me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah! But you know what? If you need Hugsy, don't worry. Emma will totally understand. I won't... but whatever .

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, wait wait wait wait a minute wait a minute, I mean Rach, I mean if if... . If Hugsy means that much to Emma then... well she can have him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oooh... you're sweet, I knew uncle Joey would step up. Look Emma, look who's baaack!
lines.stage_direction: Turns to face Emma in the the playpen

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look forget it forget it... I can't do it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you gonna... you're going to take Hugsy away from a little child?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How do you think I got him in the first place?

scenes.scene_description: Joey's room: Joey is sitting on his bed reading DooL transcript when Rachel walks in
Phoebe's apartment: Phoebe, Monica and Mike sitting on the couch

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright you two, I'm gonna go to the bathroom. Now I don't want anything going on while I'm gone. Here's a few things you can discuss: mucus, fungus and the idea of me and Ross doing it.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I've missed you so much! No, I'm not gonna ask you to get back together because I know we want different things, but just to be with you one more night.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, I want that too, but IS that going to make it too hard?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: It can't be any harder than this... I mean, If I had known the last time I saw you would be the last time, I... I would have stopped to memorize your face, the way you move, everything about you. If I had known the last time I kissed you would have been the last time... I never would have stopped.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Kiss him, you fool!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Didn't you hear that speech? If you don't kiss him then I will!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I missed you so much!

lines.speaker: Strange man
lines.text: I knew you'd be here!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh, crap!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Who is this?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: My friend Manny. I asked him to keep me away from you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi, that's what I'm doing for Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Manny
lines.text: Well, you are not doing a very good job!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Manny
lines.text: What's with the kissing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, at least I knew where my guy was.

lines.speaker: Manny
lines.text: Oh yeah yeah, thank God you were here to oversee all the kissing!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You didn't hear the speech!

lines.speaker: I've heard the speech
lines.text: "if he knew it was gonna be the last time he saw her... "

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, it was very moving! You're just heartless!

lines.speaker: Manny
lines.text: You're weak!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're... weird!

lines.speaker: Manny
lines.text: Your pants are undone!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh !
lines.stage_direction: realizes her pants are undone and zips them up

lines.speaker: Manny
lines.text: Where did they go?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Damn it!

lines.speaker: Manny
lines.text: Oh we blew it. I blame myself.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And I blame you too.

scenes.scene_description: Monica is getting up from the couch

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's been an hour and not one of my classmates has shown up! I tell you, when I actually die some people are gonna get seriously haunted!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's apartment

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There you go! Someone came!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, ok! I'm gonna go hide! Oh, this is so exciting, my first mourner!
lines.stage_direction: he hides in the bedroom and closes the door

scenes.scene_description: someone knocks on the door

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi, glad you could come.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Please, come in.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Hi, you're Chandler Bing, right? I'm Tom Gordon, I was in your class.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes, yes... let me... take your coat.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Thanks... uh... I'm so sorry about Ross, it's...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: At least he died doing what he loved... watching blimps

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who is he?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Some guy, Tom Gordon.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't remember him, but then again I touched so many lives.
lines.stage_direction: they press their ears against the door

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, did you know Ross well?

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Oh, actually I barely knew him. Yeah, I came because I heard Chandler's news. D'you know if he's seeing anyone?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, he is. Me.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: What? You... You... Oh! Can I ask you a personal question? Ho-how do you shave your beard so close?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok Tommy, that's enough mourning for you! Here we go, bye bye!!
lines.stage_direction: entering, very upset he shoves him out the door

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Hey, listen. Call me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm dead and no one cares?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I look like a man??

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Please, one ridiculous problem at a time!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It isn't ridiculous, look around! No one's here!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You gave them one day's notice, not everyone in our class checks the web site everyday and Monica... it's probably the way you stand!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, you're right. Still somebody must have seen it... I mean, I went to that school for 4 years, I didn't have an impact on anyone?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, that's not true. You had an impact on me, I mean, it's 15 years later and we're still best friends. Doesn't that count for something?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sure it's somebody for you. Now, go hide.
lines.stage_direction: Ross hides in the bedroom again

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Monica go open the door

lines.speaker: Kori
lines.text: Hi. I'm here for Ross Geller's memorial service.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Kori? Kori Weston?

lines.speaker: Kori
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! You look amazing!

lines.speaker: Kori
lines.text: And you are...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Chandler, Chandler Bing. I'm not gay, I'm not gay at all.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You are married though.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't listen to him, he's in a really bad mood!

lines.speaker: Kori
lines.text: I can't believe that Ross is gone. It is just so sad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I didn't know Ross and you were so close.

lines.speaker: Kori
lines.text: We weren't but we had one class together. He was such a great guy and he talked so passionately about science. I always remembered him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sure that would mean a lot to him. And if heaven has a door, I'm sure he's pressing his ear up against it and listening intently.

lines.speaker: Kori
lines.text: I thought so many times about calling him and asking him out. I guess I really missed my chance.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But you didn't! I'm still alive!! Kori, I know this is a big surprise for you. It's a long story but the things you just said really made my day! I mean, the fact that you are here means more to me than if this room were filled with people!
lines.stage_direction: he bounds into the lounge room

lines.speaker: Kori
lines.text: You sick freak, who does that? I can't believe I had a crush on you!
lines.stage_direction: she leaves and slams the door behind her

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did you hear that? Kori Weston had a crush on me!!

scenes.scene_description: Chandlers opens the door. A beautiful woman stands at the doorway.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No Mum, I'm not dead. I know it's not something to kid about. It was just a practical joke between Chandler and me, but it's over, ok? Actually no, even if I had died, you would not be left childless. Monica?

scenes.scene_description: Ross' apartment

title: Friends - 918 - The one with the lottery

lines.speaker: Teleplay
lines.text: Sherry Bilsing - Graham & Ellen Plummer

lines.speaker: Story
lines.text: Brian Buckner & Sebastian Jones

lines.speaker: Directed by
lines.text: Gary Halvorson

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Monica, hey, can I borrow the Porsche?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But ehm...what is it not?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A place to entertain my lady friends.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And what else is it not?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A place to eat spaghetti.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Very good! What do you need it for anyway?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well, the powerball lottery is upto 300 million and they don't sell tickets here in New York, so...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! So you're driving up to Connecticut?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah Connecticut...Not West Virginia.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, maybe I'll drive you up there! I'd like to buy some tickets myself!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah with Chandler not getting paid, we could really use 300 million dollars.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, because if I was at my old job we'd say 300 million? No thank you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey will you get me tickets too?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah me too. oh! I have an idea. Why don't we all pitch in 50 bucks, we'll pool our money together and then if we win, we'll split it!

lines.speaker: Everyone almost simultaneously except Ross
lines.text: yeah thats a great idea!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No thanks!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You don't wanna win the lottery?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh...sure I do, and I also wanna be King of my own country and find out what happened to Amelia Earhart.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Still on Amelia Earhart?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The woman just vanished!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Seriously, Ross, you don't want in on this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! Do you know what your odds are of winning the lottery? I...I mean you have a better chance of being struck by lightning 42 times.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes but there's six of us so we'd only have to get struck by lightning 7 times.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I like those odds!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Seriously you guys, I can't believe you're going to spend 250 dollars on the lottery, I mean that's such a bunch of boohaki.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll ask. Boohaki?
lines.stage_direction: looking around at the others To Ross

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh oh, we think Emma is about to start talking so we're trying to be careful about what words we use in front of her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah so get ready to hear alot of ehm...boohaki, goshdarnit and brotherpucker.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How do you know she's gonna start talking?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well when I talk to her I almost feel like she understands what I'm saying.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Kinda like Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's that now?

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk - Chandler, Monica, Phoebe, Ross and Rachel are sitting on the sofas. Joey enters

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: God! Look at all these tickets! It's so exciting! You know I haven't won anything since the sixth grade.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pie eating contest?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh! You assume because I was heavy that's the only way I could win something?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I saw a picture of you covered in blueberries.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That was a good day!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's apartment. Monica, Chandler, Rachel and Joey are in the kitchen

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They're towing your car, they're towing your car!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm parked in a garage on Morton!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They're towing a car. And I am seeing...spots.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my god Ross! You don't have Emma! And Rachel you don't have Emma! Where's Emma? Who has Emma!?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey relax! My mother picked her up two hours ago. You were there!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I was?

lines.speaker: Racel
lines.text: Yes and you talked to her...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I did?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She dropped off a casserole?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah! The casserole lady.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, did you come by to watch us win the big bucks?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, uh... and then I figured after you win, we could all go out to the balcony and see a night rainbow with gremlins dancing on top of it!

scenes.scene_description: Suddenly Ross comes running into the apartment

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't touch the phone! I'll get it, I'll get it, I'll get it!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Think he washed his hands?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello? Hey Charlie, what do you know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler is supposed to find out if he's getting an assistent job at his ad agency. But out of the 15 interns, they are only hiring three.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh! Tough odds!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah if only it were a sure thing like your 24 state lottery!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look who's coming around!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Damnit. Alright call me when you know more.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Did you get it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: One of the slots got filled.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: By you!?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sense the tone! No that kid Nate got it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh! I hate that guy! I mean come on kid! Pull up your pants!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah I know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, there's two spots left right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah...I mean I want this so much! I mean, I wanna get one, I want my friend Charlie to get one...Except I don't care about Charlie.

scenes.scene_description: phone starts ringing and Chandler comes running out of the bathroom

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey you guys! Ok, you're not going to believe this! I just saw my psychic and she said I was definitely gonna win the lottery tonight!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey that reminds me, I thought we could use some extra luck so I brought a wishbone home from work.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A psychic AND a wishbone? Guys! Give someone else a chance!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, who wants to do it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh can I? Vegetarians never get to do the wishbone. It's really not fair either! You know, just because we don't eat the meat doesn't mean we don't like to play with the carcasses!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, hey Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, I'm good, I don't wanna get that turkey smell all over my hands.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'll do it!! It'll get the casserole stink off of mine.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe enters

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I hope I win!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, it doesn't really matter ... you're both wishing for the same thing, right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't tell you what I'm wishing for! Else...you know...won't come true!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Right! .. but we "know" what you're wishing for!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Can't really say!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I understand, but you're wishing for what we think you're wishing for, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm not really comfortable with these questions!

lines.speaker: Rachel, Chandler and Monica
lines.text: Please, just do it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: One, two, three!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe and Joey both grab one side of the wishbone

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I won, hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know what, I'm sure your wish is gonna come true, but, you guys - just in case, maybe a genie will come out if we rub this lamp! Ah!! That thing gets hot!!
lines.stage_direction: rubs lamp, stops because it's very hot

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know, Ross, just keep making your jokes. How are you gonna feel if we actually do win?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, you're not gonna win.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I know, I know, the odds are against us, but somebody has to win, and it could be us! And then how you gonna feel? You know, we're gonna be all like "oh everybody, let's take our helicopters up to the cape" and you're gonna be all like "oh, I can't guys, I'll meet you guys up there, I gotta gas up the Hyundai"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, I've heard myself on tape and I sound nothing like that.

lines.speaker: I can see the headline now
lines.text: "Lottery winners' friend filled with regret eats own arm".

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why would I eat my own arm?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well you wouldn't, but we own the paper, we can print whatever we want.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know what, Ross? I'm gonna throw in 50 bucks for you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because I know that you think the lottery is "boohaki" but we're all here and gonna watch the numbers and have fun. And you're my brother, and I want you to be a part of this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You don't have to do that, I'll pay for myself. But just the fact that you want me to have fun with you guys - that's so sweet! Come here

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Get a room!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok, so now that you're in, what are you gonna do if we win?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know, probably just invest it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh! Calm down ...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Seriously, that's your fantasy? To invest it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry, did I say "invest it"? I meant "be cool and piss it all away"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, ooh, I know! We should pool all own money and buy the Knicks!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't really care about the Knicks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, you will when I pick you as starting forward.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You would do that? I never get picked!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I'm not sure a sports team is the way to go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're not gonna let me buy the Knicks?? I can't believe you're taking this away from me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're right, it has been you dream for over 15 seconds.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, how long until they announce the numbers, Mommy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll take this one too Uh ... Mommy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I've gotten into the habit of calling Rachel "Mommy" when we're around Emma. Which I now realize we are not ...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm hoping that if she hears it enough it will be her first word.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Although if we're gonna do that, we should probably call me "Daddy" too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oooh, I like that, "daddy"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I ... I was just talking about Rachel.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oooh, is daddy getting angry? Is daddy gonna spank me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well that depends ... have you been a baaad gi .. no I can't.
lines.stage_direction: tries to be sexy too stops

scenes.scene_description: they break the wishbone

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello? Hello? Oh, hey Charlie. Did anybody else hear? ... What? Susan got it?? How? Oh man, I would have slept with him!! .. Alright, bye.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, I'm sorry. But hey, there's one spot left, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well no, Charlie's gonna get that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, alright look, just uh... just try to remember how you felt when you were in love, and think about that when you're playing the scene.

lines.text: Oh! ok, yeah, I think I can do that. Yeah ok, there's this party scene coming up.. and Olivia and her husband are there and all Drake wants to do is grab her and kiss her, but he can't... And that makes me think about all those times when I wanted to grab you and kiss you, but you didn't know so I would just pretend everything was cool, but really, it was killing me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, you never..you never talked about that before...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well.. hey, you know what else I could use? There's a scene where Drake sneaks into Olivia's bedroom, and she doesn't know he's there - which never happened with us! And he knows he shouldn't be there, but he just wants to look at her... you know? And I remember all those mornings before you even put on your make-up, when I would think to myself, my God, she...is... beautiful... and it hurts so much, cuz I knew I could never tell you but it was worth it just to be there looking at you.

lines.text: Can I ask you a question?

lines.text: uh-huh

lines.text: And I couldn't find this little plastic thing that goes on top of the blender...and I thought...well... how important can that be, right...? Turns out very!

lines.text: Wow...definitely just Drake...

lines.speaker: Rachel

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A little. Yeah no, I really am worried, you know, I mean I have to make it convincing that I'm in love with Olivia.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So...I've never played that!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh! Honey, it can't be that hard, I mean, you've been in love before?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh...well...just once...with you...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok...this could be a little awkward...I'm just going to blow past it... well can't you just use that method actor thing where you use your real life memories to help you in your performance?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What the hell are you talking about??
lines.stage_direction: looks at her for a moment

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, alright look, just uh... just try to remember how you felt when you were in love, and think about that when you're playing the scene.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! ok, yeah, I think I can do that. Yeah ok, there's this party scene coming up.. and Olivia and her husband are there and all Drake wants to do is grab her and kiss her, but he can't... And that makes me think about all those times when I wanted to grab you and kiss you, but you didn't know so I would just pretend everything was cool, but really, it was killing me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, you never..you never talked about that before...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well.. hey, you know what else I could use? There's a scene where Drake sneaks into Olivia's bedroom, and she doesn't know he's there - which never happened with us! And he knows he shouldn't be there, but he just wants to look at her... you know? And I remember all those mornings before you even put on your make-up, when I would think to myself, my God, she...is... beautiful... and it hurts so much, cuz I knew I could never tell you but it was worth it just to be there looking at you.

lines.text: Wow, do you mean like kiss him-kiss him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah! I mean, that was pretty intense.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What do you think brought than on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know! I mean, maybe that's something to do with the fact that I saw him do a love scene yesterday.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: A love scene? With who?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Olivia.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Olivia? I thought she was marrying Connor! Oh right, real life more important.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So do you think that my dream means anything?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know. I mean, you saw him do a love scene, so maybe you don't have a thing for Joey, maybe you have a thing for Drake.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah! Well it was Joey reading Drake's lines in the dream...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course it was! Trust me, when it comes to psychology I know what I'm talking about. I took two psych classes in college.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You took the same class twice.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It was hard!

lines.text: Excuse me, excuse me, hi, I'm Monica Geller..I'm the head chef here.. .. Ok, I was actually expecting a little applause there, but whatever! Ok, quick question: by a show of hands, how many of you were bothered by this woman's singing outside?

lines.text: Ok, well, who identified the tone of this restaurant as pretentious comma garlicky?

lines.text: Ok who thinks the food is delicious and a little pretention never hurt anyone?

lines.text: Ok, well, alright, who thinks the food is fine, the music was fine, but your evening was ruined by this incessant poll taking? .

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Excuse us! Alright here's a question: Who was so worried about her restaurant being fancy that she made a big deal about her friend playing her music and feels really bad about it now?
lines.stage_direction: then to Phoebe raises her hand

lines.text: Oh...Who was so stupid and stubborn that she lashed out against her friend's cooking which she actually thinks is pretty great!

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: Joey, do you have peanut butter on the back of your head?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How...how...?

lines.text: I was making a peanut butter smoothy, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: uh-huh

lines.text: And I couldn't find this little plastic thing that goes on top of the blender...and I thought...well... how important can that be, right...? Turns out very!

lines.text: Wow...definitely just Drake...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.text: What...how is it going with Drake?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh...I don't think it's going very well...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What...that scene I saw was so good!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I'm feeling really insecure about the one we are shooting tomorrow...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, is this that thing that you do when you say you're bad so I'll give you a compliment?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A little. Yeah no, I really am worried, you know, I mean I have to make it convincing that I'm in love with Olivia.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So...I've never played that!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh! Honey, it can't be that hard, I mean, you've been in love before?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh...well...just once...with you...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok...this could be a little awkward...I'm just going to blow past it... well can't you just use that method actor thing where you use your real life memories to help you in your performance?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What the hell are you talking about??
lines.stage_direction: looks at her for a moment

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, alright look, just uh... just try to remember how you felt when you were in love, and think about that when you're playing the scene.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! ok, yeah, I think I can do that. Yeah ok, there's this party scene coming up.. and Olivia and her husband are there and all Drake wants to do is grab her and kiss her, but he can't... And that makes me think about all those times when I wanted to grab you and kiss you, but you didn't know so I would just pretend everything was cool, but really, it was killing me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, you never..you never talked about that before...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well.. hey, you know what else I could use? There's a scene where Drake sneaks into Olivia's bedroom, and she doesn't know he's there - which never happened with us! And he knows he shouldn't be there, but he just wants to look at her... you know? And I remember all those mornings before you even put on your make-up, when I would think to myself, my God, she...is... beautiful... and it hurts so much, cuz I knew I could never tell you but it was worth it just to be there looking at you.
