lines.text: Thank god, it's just you! I thought someone was swinging a bag of cats against the wall.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You'd better get back in that kitchen Monica, the garlic is not gonna overuse itself.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, you have to stop playing now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why? The only person my playing is bothering is you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah? Ok, let's settle this, come on!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Get your garlic-peelers off me!
lines.stage_direction: while Monica drags her in the restaurant

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Excuse me, excuse me, hi, I'm Monica Geller..I'm the head chef here.. .. Ok, I was actually expecting a little applause there, but whatever! Ok, quick question: by a show of hands, how many of you were bothered by this woman's singing outside?
lines.stage_direction: to the restuarant customers pauses as if waiting for something a few people raise their hands

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, ok, how many of you enjoyed the music outside? Ha!
lines.stage_direction: a few people raise their hands

lines.speaker: Alright, let me ask you this question
lines.text: How many of you thought the music was fine, but not in keeping with the tone of the restaurant? .
lines.stage_direction: a few raise their hands again

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, well, who identified the tone of this restaurant as pretentious comma garlicky?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok who thinks the food is delicious and a little pretention never hurt anyone?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, well, alright, who thinks the food is fine, the music was fine, but your evening was ruined by this incessant poll taking? .

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Excuse us! Alright here's a question: Who was so worried about her restaurant being fancy that she made a big deal about her friend playing her music and feels really bad about it now?
lines.stage_direction: then to Phoebe raises her hand

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh...Who was so stupid and stubborn that she lashed out against her friend's cooking which she actually thinks is pretty great!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry too...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: ooohh... hey! Wanna stick around and I'll whip you up some dinner?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! As long as it's free! Food here is ridiculously over-p...

scenes.scene_description: Monica comes out

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Who's hoping the hand raising thing is still cute enough that you won't hate me?

scenes.scene_description: Monica seems to take it amiss

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, do you have peanut butter on the back of your head?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh man! I thought I got it all!
lines.stage_direction: Touches the back of his head and licks his fingers

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How...how...?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I was making a peanut butter smoothy, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: uh-huh

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And I couldn't find this little plastic thing that goes on top of the blender...and I thought...well... how important can that be, right...? Turns out very!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow...definitely just Drake...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What...how is it going with Drake?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh...I don't think it's going very well...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What...that scene I saw was so good!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I'm feeling really insecure about the one we are shooting tomorrow...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, is this that thing that you do when you say you're bad so I'll give you a compliment?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A little. Yeah no, I really am worried, you know, I mean I have to make it convincing that I'm in love with Olivia.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So...I've never played that!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh! Honey, it can't be that hard, I mean, you've been in love before?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh...well...just once...with you...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok...this could be a little awkward...I'm just going to blow past it... well can't you just use that method actor thing where you use your real life memories to help you in your performance?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What the hell are you talking about??
lines.stage_direction: looks at her for a moment

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, alright look, just uh... just try to remember how you felt when you were in love, and think about that when you're playing the scene.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! ok, yeah, I think I can do that. Yeah ok, there's this party scene coming up.. and Olivia and her husband are there and all Drake wants to do is grab her and kiss her, but he can't... And that makes me think about all those times when I wanted to grab you and kiss you, but you didn't know so I would just pretend everything was cool, but really, it was killing me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, you never..you never talked about that before...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well.. hey, you know what else I could use? There's a scene where Drake sneaks into Olivia's bedroom, and she doesn't know he's there - which never happened with us! And he knows he shouldn't be there, but he just wants to look at her... you know? And I remember all those mornings before you even put on your make-up, when I would think to myself, my God, she...is... beautiful... and it hurts so much, cuz I knew I could never tell you but it was worth it just to be there looking at you.
lines.stage_direction: In a romantic voice Rachel looks very moved pauses, while looking at her with sentiment

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's apartment, Rachel walks in

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks dude!!!This is GREAT!!
lines.stage_direction: excited leaves Rachel very touched on the couch and goes into his room

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel continue to look at each other in silence for a while

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I got you something from Vermont!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Besides tampons and salt? Ooh! My God! Maple candy! That's so sweet of you. That's weird...it's empty!
lines.stage_direction: without looking up from what she's doing Then looks Opens the box

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's apartement, Monica is at the kitchen table writing something and Chandler enters kitchen area

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi you guys! what's going on, you... you guys wanna hang out...or...? do you...do you guys hear a buzzing?
lines.stage_direction: sounds excited Looks around the room nervously

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I didn't factor in the room tax.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh dude, don't worry about it, I found an unattended maid's car. We're way ahead of the game.

scenes.scene_description: Then Ross enters

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my god.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There's something new in the bowl.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, we have enough, just walk away.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, but I want...I want the pinecones!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There's a forest right outside.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's not the same.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok, go quick!

scenes.scene_description: they make as if to go, but Ross notices something

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you for a delightful stay.

scenes.scene_description: Ross starts stuffing pinecones in the suitcase. As the receptionist walks in, Chandler makes a bird's verse and Ross stops

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, my maple candy!

scenes.scene_description: he picks up the suitcase, which falls open, revealing all the stuff they have taken from the hotel. The receptionist just looks at them.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright, alright look, just uh... just try to remember how you felt when you were in love, and think about that when you're playing the scene.

lines.text: Oh! ok, yeah, I think I can do that. Yeah ok, there's this party scene coming up.. and Olivia and her husband are there and all Drake wants to do is grab her and kiss her, but he can't... And that makes me think about all those times when I wanted to grab you and kiss you, but you didn't know so I would just pretend everything was cool, but really, it was killing me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, you never..you never talked about that before...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well.. hey, you know what else I could use? There's a scene where Drake sneaks into Olivia's bedroom, and she doesn't know he's there - which never happened with us! title: Friends - 920 - The one with the soap opera party

lines.speaker: Teleplay
lines.text: Andrew Reich & Ted Cohen

lines.speaker: Story
lines.text: Shana Goldberg-Meehan

lines.speaker: Directed by
lines.text: Sheldon Epps

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, you guys, what are you doing tomorrow night?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, let me see... I-I believe I'm... yes, falling asleep in front of the TV.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, my agent hooked me up with six tickets to a great play.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I could fall asleep at a play.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's a one-woman play called "Why don't you like me: a bitter woman's journey through life".

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It sounds interesting!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, it does sound interesting, I mean, to listen to a woman complain for two hours, I don't think it gets bett...
lines.stage_direction: Ross starts snoring, faking to fall asleep

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, I know, we can drive, we can vote, we can work, what more do these broads want?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You guys are gonna have a great time, I promise!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? How come that you don't have to go!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I wish I could but I just found out that I have to be at work really early the next day, so I can't go, but, you know, take the extra ticket and invite whoever you want.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, let's see, who do I hate?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, sorry... Oops, sorry.
lines.stage_direction: gets up from the sofa and moves to the kitchen but Joey blocks her way

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, here you go.
lines.stage_direction: lifts Rachel up and moves her behind him so she can walk on

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh... oooh... oh, ah... Can I ask you a question?
lines.stage_direction: pause Rachel is all bah-jiggity about Joey pause to Monica

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you think it's possible for two friends to fool around and... and not have it be a big deal?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I don't think it ever works. Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No reason.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, no, Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who do you wanna fool around with?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nobody, forget it!

scenes.scene_description: Monica's apartment

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Maybe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You can't!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why? Seriously I did not understand a word that you said.
lines.stage_direction: whispering Monica gesticulates mumbling something that starts with "because"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: In the hall.

scenes.scene_description: Monica points at Joey, Rachel turns and sees him

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You wanna fool around with Joey?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! You know, ever since I had that dream about him, and can't get it out of my head! And what's the big deal, people do it all the time!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who? Who do you know that are friends that just fool around?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, off the top of my head... Don and Janet.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who, who are they?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know them from work.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Both of them?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, one of them...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Which one?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, what were the names I just said?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, things can get incredibly complicated.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, all right, you're right , I won't do anything with Joey, I just thought that we Ok so that would be two cups of tarragon, one pound of baking soda and one red onion?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What the hell are you cooking!

lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, you guys won't believe what I have to do for work today.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but, Ross you chose a career of talking about dinosaurs.

title: Friends - The one with the fertility test

lines.speaker: Teleplay
lines.text: Robert Carlock

lines.speaker: Story
lines.text: Scott Silveri

lines.speaker: Directed by
lines.text: Gary Halvorson

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's so weird, how did Joey end up kissing Charlie last night? I thought you'd end up kissing Charlie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I thought I'd end up kissing Charlie too ok? But SURPRISE !

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I missed most of the party Charlie's a girl, right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, she is this new professor of my department that I did not kiss.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know why Joey had to kiss her! I mean, of all the girls at the party, GOD!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why do you care so much?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes Rachel, why do you care so much?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Be-cause Ross is the father of my child! You know... and I... want him to hook up with lots of women! I just... All I'm saying is... I don't think that Joey and Charlie have anything in common.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I don't know, they seem to have a shared interest in each other's tonsils...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, Joey and a professor! Can you imagine if they had kids and if the kids got her intelligence and Joey's raw sexual magnetism... Oh, those nerds will get laaaaaid !

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, so... Ross, you're ok with all this? I mean...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, it's no big deal. I mean, I just met her and I'm fine with it...

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, God. I forgot how hot she was!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm gonna get some more coffee.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh, you know what? I'll come with you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, a professor, uh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! She is cool, and she's so smart! Her mind is totally acrimonious . That's not how she used it...?
lines.stage_direction: which, being Joey, he mispronounces "amonious" pause

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I feel like I owe you an explanation. I don't ordinarily go around kissing guys at parties. I'm... well, I'm kind of embarrassed. I really hope you don't think less of me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhm no! Think less of you! No, I don't think less of you. I mean, you saw someone you liked and you kissed them. I mean, those people who like someone and don't kiss them... those-those people are stupid, I hate those people.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: You know, actually I'm a little surprised to myself. I mean, Joey is so different from the guys I usually date. I mean, they're all professors, and intellectuals, and paleontologists mostly, you know, very cerebral...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I know the type.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, if you wanna grab a bite before work we'd better get acrimonious. No? Am I getting close?

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Charlie enter. Ross looks at her

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey you guys! Look what I just got.
lines.stage_direction: she shows them a pair of slippers

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, OH! Wow, I love those! Where did you get them?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I bought them off Ebay! They used to belong to the late Shania Twain.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, Shania Twain is still alive!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh... then I overpaid.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, what's this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it's a gift certificate to this new SPA in SOHO.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, you can't show Phoebe this! She hates those corporate massage chains.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah, why, now I can't get a massage? There are so many things that she disapproves of! I can't eat veal, I can't wear fur, I can't go hunting...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you wanna go hunting?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I would like to have the option!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What's up? Hey, Rachel!!
lines.stage_direction: coming back from the bathroom she sees the gift certificate in Rachel's hands

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, you can't go there! You know how I feel about these "big massage places"! They're putting people like me out of business!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And she wants to go hunting, too!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, come on, I don't wanna waste it! It would be like throwing away a hundred bucks!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, this is not about the MONEY , ok? It's about... it's about corporate greed destroying our hearts and leaving us... the hollow shells.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't care about any of that!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, do you care about friendship?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I feel really strongly about this, Rachel. Please, don't use this gift certificate. I'm asking you as a friend.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, not as a friend, Phoebe!! Fine, I won't use it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Promise?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I promise.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you.
lines.stage_direction: she tears up the gift certificate

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But I am going hunting!!

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's apartment

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey honey! I missed you today!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. What d'you wanna do tonight?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, well... Maybe we could...
lines.stage_direction: he sweeps the stuff off the table and wordlessly invites Monica to have sex on it

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, trying to turn me on by making a mess? Know your audience! Besides, tomorrow we're doing those fertility tests and until then you need to keep your tadpoles in the tank.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We really need to take those tests?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, we've been trying to have a baby for over a year. I think it's a good idea to find out if everything's ok. Just a few routine tests.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But I don't wanna do it in a cup!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is the big deal?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's weird! In a doctor's office?

scenes.location: Joey's apartment. Joey is sitting on his bed and the phone rings

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey! Hey, I need to talk to Charlie. Is she there?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. no... eh... she went shopping with Rachel. Why? What's up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm meeting with professor Sherman about my being the keynote speaker...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! How's it going?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It could be better! He, uhm... he fell asleep!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What!? But I already bought my ticket to Bermuda!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Barbados.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine, I'll rent a car and drive...! Ross, you have to get that job!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What am I supposed to do? He's out cold! In fact he was just talking in his sleep before and evidently he wants someone named Fran to spank him harder.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, just wake him up!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't! If he realizes that I'm the one that put him to sleep, I won't get the job!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh! That's a tough one. Oh! Wait a minute, this happened to me before! Yeah, I was auditioning for a play and the producer fell asleep and... no wait a minute... it was me who fell asleep... Yeah I mean hey, Shakespeare, how about a chase scene once in a while!?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey guys! Dinner's ready!

lines.speaker: Zack
lines.text: Oh! I'm gonna go wash up first. Thanks!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So what do you think? I want that guys genes for my kid! Those eyes, those cheeckbones!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, there's enthusiastic and there's just plain gay !!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don't like him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think he is fine! It's just that we don't know anything real about him... we should get more information.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright! Just follow my lead!

lines.speaker: Zack
lines.text: You guys have such a great place here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh! Thanks, I'm crazy about our place. Hey! speaking of crazy... do you have a history of mental illness in the family?

lines.text: Uhm... no. Although I did have an uncle who voted for Dukakis.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's really not the kind of thing we are looking for Zack.

lines.speaker: Zack
lines.text: Okaaay... so eh... so tell me, how did you guys meet.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, friends first, drunk in London, you know the story. I've got a better question for you: Do you or any of your blood relatives have diabetes?

lines.speaker: Zack
lines.text: No...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Eh... Heart Disease, Alzheimers, gout?

lines.speaker: Zack
lines.text: You guys don't have people for dinner a lot, huh?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're just making conversation.

lines.speaker: Zack
lines.text: Ok. I heard a joke today. It's pretty funny...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know what's not funny? Male Pattern Baldness

lines.speaker: Zack
lines.text: Ok listen, you guys have shown a lot of interest in me tonight and I'm flattered and... and quite frankly a little frightened. Can we just talk about something else?

lines.text: Sure! Alright...

lines.text: Ravioli's delicious!

lines.text: No I didn't.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yess!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We're teeth people Zack!

lines.text: Alright! Let's just do it. Let's just go over there and see if she heard.

lines.text: Good plan.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok. Wha...? where? Where are you going?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! I'm sorry Rachel, I don't have time for your childish games, ok? I still have to go find something incredible to wear so I can beat Mike at "who's more over who"!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, hi! Hey, where've you been?

lines.text: Oh! trying on clothes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Wi... in the dres... in the dressing room!? Well, that's so weird! Phoebe and I were just trying on clothes in the dressing room. God it's just such a small world!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Rachel... I heard you guys whispering.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God. You did. You heard. Ok, listen, let me explain.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: No! There's nothing to explain. I heard you. Phoebe likes Joey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: It's just that... I don't understand it... I mean, Phoebe likes Joey and then she comes here to buy a dress to impress another guy...?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! That's Phoebe. That's Phoebe. You know, she just wants them all! It's like she's a nympho!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: You know, by the way. I heard you tell her not to do anything. Thanks for sticking up for me. You are such a nice person.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I try...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! You look... ... stop-eating hot! Which is like the highest level of hotness!

lines.text: Mike knows I'm coming, and if I don't show up he'll think it's because of him! And I don't want to lose face! That's a very serious thing in my culture.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, then you go to that party and you pretend to be over Mike. And afterward you come to my place and I'll get you good and drunk!

lines.speaker: Phoebe

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: David?

scenes.scene_description: Outside Central Perk.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Phoebe! Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Wow, you look unbelievable.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. What-what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well, I'm back from Minsk... permanently.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well, remember how I was trying to achieve the positronic distillation of subatomic particles?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well, after eight years of research I discovered that it can't be done.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, it's great that you're back! How are you?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Good, good, life is good...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Ah well, I-I'm seeing someone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, good for you.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: She's also a scientist, so she's very smart and pretty and... well, it's actually because of you, really, that we're together, I mean, I saw what you had with that Mike guy, and I just said "Boy, I want that".

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mike and I broke up.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: You're kidding me. Because I'm not seeing anybody, I've just totally made that up.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah, I don't know why, I'm sorry, I guess I just didn't want to lose face.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I understand. Yeah. Ok so then ok, so we're both living in New York, not seeing anyone. That's so not like us!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah, I know. Well... this is probably a stupid question, seeing that you look like that, but do you have some place that you need to be right now?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well... no.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Do you wanna get a drink?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'd love to.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Do you smell beets?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, got it, stay upwind of me.

lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think we've found our sperm!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Does seem pretty perfect.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, you think so, well? Should I ask him?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why not, just because his great-grandmother was obese, our kids are gonna get that from you anyway!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, that's not it. It's just that when we were asking him all those questions before, I just... I just realized I don't care if he's the most perfect guy in the world... he's not you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, he's better!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, he's not. And if I can't get pregnant with you, then I don't want to get pregnant by... him or anyone else.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really? Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank God, because I don't wanna do this either. You know, I was just doing because I thought that was what you wanted to do. You know, I'm the husband, I'm supposed to... bring the sperm.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That is so sweet. I love you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you know this leaves us with...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Adoption.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How do you feel about that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think I feel ok about it. Actually I think I feel really good about it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Me too. I wanna find a baby that needs a home and I wanna raise it with you. And I wanna mess it up in our own specific way.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So this is it, we're really gonna adopt?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, we're gonna be parents!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We are gonna be great parents.

lines.speaker: And it could be soon. I mean, think about it
lines.text: right now, somewhere out there our baby could be being conceived.
lines.stage_direction: they go look through the window

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait, if we're lucky, and we're really really really quiet, we may be able to hear the sound of a condom breaking!

they hug

lines.text: No you didn't.

lines.text: Really?

lines.text: No you did.

I had to pull some strings but I was able to get everyone passes to the entire conference! That's right! This babies will get you into all the paleontology lectures and seminars.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Hum, so, I started to say you something earlier, hum... There was another reason I realized it was time to end it with Joey. I kind of realized I... was starting to have feelings... for someone else.

lines.text: Oh. Can I... can I ask who?

scenes.location: Hotel's bar. Ross is running to Charlie trying not to be seen with two cocktails in his hands. She's hidden behind a huge plant

lines.text: I'm sorry... we... we can't.

lines.text: All right, all right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean, you just went out with my best friend, and I just think it'd be a really really bad idea. Or-or not!

scenes.scene_description: Charlie kisses Ross, they stop for a moment and then he kisses her back

scenes.location: Hotel's bar. Ross is running to Charlie trying not to be seen with two cocktails in his hands. She's hidden behind a huge plant

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

scenes.scene_description: Joey walks in and sees Ross and Charlie kissing. He gives a faint, rueful smile, then he seems to recollect something and suddenly he moves back to Rachel's room. He knocks on her door and she opens

scenes.location: Hotel's bar. Ross is running to Charlie trying not to be seen with two cocktails in his hands. She's hidden behind a huge plant
scenes.scene_description: Joey says nothing, but enters the room and kisses her. They are kissing passionately only to stop for a brief "oh" from Rachel. They continue their passionate kiss and Joey closes the door with his foot and it shuts in the camera's "face". And that's the end of the ninth season.

title: Friends - 923-924 - The one in Barbados

lines.speaker: Part 1 written by
lines.text: Shana Goldberg-Meehan & Scott Silveri

lines.speaker: Part 2 written by
lines.text: Marta Kauffman & David Crane

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! I'm all packed and ready to go!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, that's right! Daddy and uncle Joey are going on a trip today. We're going to a conference in Barbados, right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Mmh-mmh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can you say Barbados?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Barbados!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, I gotta say. I mean, it means so much to me that you guys are coming all the way over there to hear me do my speech! UH! And I've a surprise, uh... I had to pull some strings but I was able to get everyone passes to the entire conference! That's right! This babies will get you into all the paleontology lectures and seminars.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you have anything that would... get us out of them?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah Ross, I mean... we're excited to hear the speech but the rest of the time we're gonna wanna do, you know, "island's stuff".

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I think David would probably wanna hear a few lectures.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, right, because he's a scientist!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, because, you know, he's been in Minsk for 8 years and if he gets too much direct sunlight, he'll die.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, we gotta go, yeah? So, we'll see you guys tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, let's do it! 5 hour flight with Charlie, have a couple of drinks, get under that blanket and do what comes naturally.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's a blanket Joe, not a cloak of invisibility!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Wow! This place is beautiful!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look at all these paleontologists!!

lines.text: I know, there are gonna be some pasty folks by the pool tomorrow!

lines.text: Oh my God, I can't believe you're here!

lines.text: I think I've been recognized, this happens all the time!

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Doctor Geller, I'm such a huge fan!

lines.text: That... never happens...

lines.text: Wow! This is very flattering, uh...

lines.text: Uh, uh... Sure! Uhm... "Dear..."

lines.text: "... Sarah. I dig you", Uh? "Doctor Ross Geller".

lines.text: Thank you so much!

lines.text: Yeah, oh and Sarah... I'd like to introduce you to my colleague, uh, Professor Wheeler, a-and this is Joey Tribbiani.

lines.text: Are you a paleontologist?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, God, no! No! No no, I'm an actor. You'd probably recognize me from a little show called "The Days of Our Lives".

lines.text: Dude, it's just "Days of Our Lives"... there's no the .

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, Ross! It's... It's fun, yeah! No, I-I play Doctor Drake Ramoray.

lines.text: I'm sorry, I don't own a TV.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You don't own a TV? What's all your furniture pointed at??

lines.text: Oh, certainly. That's a combination of Bernoulli's principle and Newton's third law of motion.

lines.text: See?

lines.text: Yeah, that's the same as "it has something to do with wind".

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mike ?? Who's Mike?

lines.text: Mike is your ex... uh... boyfriend!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's right! Oh, yeah... Well, I've totally forgotten about im! AH! That's-That's... a blast from the past!

lines.text: It's ok. Ho-honest mistake.

lines.text: Really, it doesn't mean anything. I mean, you know, Monica refers to Chandler as Richard all the time!

lines.text: She does?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let's get you out of here!!

lines.text: At least you took me down with you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sooo sorry!! I just... I keep thinking about Mike! I'm crazy about David, and we're having so much fun together. Why-Why do I miss Mike? That's-that's gonna go away, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess, in time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean, my feelings for Richard are certainly gone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You just did it again. Chandler , your feelings for Chandler are certainly gone!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well, Phoebe's still pretty hung up on that Mike, uh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I wouldn't read too much into it.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Still you know, a girl calls you by your ex-boyfriend's name, that-that's not a good thing, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: David, let me stop you there 'cause I think I see where this is going. I'm not very good at giving advice. So if you want advice, go to Ross, Monica, or... Joey, if the thing you wanna advice about is pizza toppings or burning sensation when you pee.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Sorry, I just... I wish there was something I could do, you know? Well, you know Phoebe...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Seriously, we're gonna do this?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I'm sorry, uh... I just wish I could make her forget about Mike already, you know... Why did Phoebe and Mike break up?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, because his penis was too big. Oh, I'm sorry, that's the kind of thing I do. They broke up because Mike didn't want to get married. Hey, what if you just let Phoebe know you'd be open to marriage?
lines.stage_direction: he notices that David is not amused pause

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: That's great! That's great! I-I'll propose to her!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well, I was probably going to do it at some point.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I didn't mean now ...

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Why not? It's brilliant! Goodbye Mike, we'll see you at the wedding, fella! well, we probably won't invite you to the wedding... Thank you, Chandler. Sincerely.
lines.stage_direction: talking to an imaginary Mike pause to Chandler

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you're welcome! Glad I could help.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: How do you think I should propose?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: David, I'm pretending to read here!!

lines.text: Wow! That Mike thing was interesting! I don't know what's gonna happen with Phoebe and David.

lines.text: I do! Want a hint? huh? "I do" "I do".

lines.text: Be-cause, we were talking about ways that he could beat Mike and I told him that Phoebe wanted to get married.

lines.text: They've only been going out for a few weeks and Phoebe is completely hung up on Mike! She'll say "No", David's heart will be broken, it will be too hard for them to recover from and then Phoebe will end up alone again .

lines.text: Man, that's some bad advice!

lines.text: Not Joey.

lines.text: Yeah, well, being a failed scientist doesn't pay quite as well as you might think. That's uhm... one seventieth of a karat. And the clarity is uhm... is quite poor.

lines.text: Ok, my husband just gave your boyfriend some very bad advice. Look, David is going to propose to you tonight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow? Really? That's fantastic!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you serious? You wanna marry him? Wha... What about Mike?

lines.text: Oh, ok, you want me to marry Mike? Alright, well, let's just gag him and handcuff him and force

lines.speaker: him down the aisle. I can just see it
lines.text: "Mike, do you take Phoebe..." You know, it's every girl's dream!

lines.text: Do you really think marrying someone else is the right answer?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure! Look, ok, bottom line: I love Mike... David ! David. I love David. Don't look at me that way, Roseanne Rosannadanna!

lines.text: By using CT scans and computer imaging we can in a very real way, bring the Mesozoic era into the 21st century.

lines.text: It's great. You're gonna be the hit of the conference.

lines.text: Oh and you know what, it will be even better tomorrow, because I won't be constantly interrupted by Joey checking to see if they put chocolates on my pillow yet. .

lines.text: Your computer, I don't know wha... everything's gone!

lines.text: Wha... what do you mean?

lines.text: What, oh my God. What did you do?

lines.text: Someone I don't know sent me an e-mail and I opened it.

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.text: Yeah, well you should! I mean, nude pictures of Anna Kournikova? I mean, she's never even won a major tournament!

lines.text: Well, I tried Billy Jean King, but... you know, you and Monica have the same "I'm gonna kill you" look...? I can usually make it go away by kissing her...

lines.text: Get out!

lines.text: You know, this happens all the time to my computer at work.

lines.text: Well, I usually go... play Tetris on somebody else's computer.

lines.text: I can't believe this. I can't believe this is happening. I have to give the keynote speech tomorrow! Ok? I have to stand up in front of all these people. What am I gonna say?

lines.text: Hey don't worry about that! I mean, Ross needs you! And Rachel and I will stay and help anyway we can.

lines.text: Ugh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright, ok, let's do it. Uhm, I know we start by discussing the shortcomings of carbon dating... uhm, and then, then I move on to what is clearly the defining moment of the Mesozoic era, the breakup of Pangea, hello! And then, there's the... eh... there's the overview of the Triassic.
